title: Numbers 1
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Numbers
paragraphs: 1 On the first day of the second month of the second year after the people of Israel had left Egypt, the LORD spoke to Moses. He spoke in the Tent of Meeting in the Desert of Sinai. He said:
2 "Count all the people of Israel by their family groups and families. List every man by name, one by one.
3 You and Aaron must count all the men who are twenty years old or older. Count them by their army groups. Count every man who can join Israel's army.
4 One man from each tribe must help you. He must be the head of his family.
5 These are the names of the men who will help you: From the tribe of Reuben, Elizur son of Shedeur;
6 from Simeon, Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai;
7 from Judah, Nahshon son of Amminadab;
8 from Issachar, Nethanel son of Zuar;
9 from Zebulun, Eliab son of Helon;
10 from the sons of Joseph: from Ephraim, Elishama son of Ammihud, and from Manasseh, Gamaliel son of Pedahzur;
11 from Benjamin, Abidan son of Gideoni;
12 from Dan, Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai;
13 from Asher, Pagiel son of Ocran;
14 from Gad, Eliasaph son of Deuel;
15 and from Naphtali, Ahira son of Enan."
16 These men were chosen from the people. They were leaders of their fathers' tribes. They were heads of the family groups of Israel.
17 So Moses and Aaron took these men whom God had named.

18 On the first day of the second month, they called all the people together. They wrote down their family records by family groups and families. They counted the names one by one of those twenty years old or older,
19 just as the LORD had told Moses. So Moses counted them in the Desert of Sinai:
20 From the sons of Reuben, the first son of Israel, by the records of their family groups and families, they counted one by one every male twenty years old or older who could join the army.
21 The men listed under the tribe of Reuben were 46,500.
22 From the sons of Simeon, by the records of their family groups and families, they counted one by one every male twenty years old or older who could join the army.
23 The men listed under the tribe of Simeon were 59,300.
24 From the sons of Gad, by the records of their family groups and families, they counted the names of all men twenty years old or older who could join the army.
25 The men listed under the tribe of Gad were 45,650.
26 From the sons of Judah, by the records of their family groups and families, they counted the names of all men twenty years old or older who could join the army.
27 The men listed under the tribe of Judah were 74,600.
28 From the sons of Issachar, by the records of their family groups and families, they counted the names of all men twenty years old or older who could join the army.
29 The men listed under the tribe of Issachar were 54,400.
30 From the sons of Zebulun, by the records of their family groups and families, they counted the names of all men twenty years old or older who could join the army.
31 The men listed under the tribe of Zebulun were 57,400.
32 From the sons of Joseph:

33 From the sons of Ephraim, by the records of their family groups and families, they counted the names of all men twenty years old or older who could join the army. The men listed under the tribe of Ephraim were 40,500.
34 And from the sons of Manasseh, by the records of their family groups and families, they counted the names of all men twenty years old or older who could join the army.
35 The men listed under the tribe of Manasseh were 32,200.
36 From the sons of Benjamin, by the records of their family groups and families, they counted the names of all men twenty years old or older who could join the army.
37 The men listed under the tribe of Benjamin were 35,400.
38 From the sons of Dan, by the records of their family groups and families, they counted the names of all men twenty years old or older who could join the army.
39 The men listed under the tribe of Dan were 62,700.
40 From the sons of Asher, by the records of their family groups and families, they counted the names of all men twenty years old or older who could join the army.
41 The men listed under the tribe of Asher were 41,500.
42 From the sons of Naphtali, by the records of their family groups and families, they counted the names of all men twenty years old or older who could join the army.
43 The men listed under the tribe of Naphtali were 53,400.
44 Moses and Aaron counted these men. The twelve leaders of Israel helped them. Each leader stood for his own family.
45 So they counted all the men of Israel twenty years old or older. These were the men who could join Israel's army. They counted them by their families.
46 The total number of men was 603,550.
47 But they did not count the Levites with the others by their fathers' tribe.
48 For the LORD had said to Moses:
49 "Do not count the tribe of Levi with the rest of the people of Israel.

50 Instead, put the Levites in charge of the tabernacle of the Testimony. Put them in charge of all its tools and everything in it. They will carry the tabernacle and all its parts. They will care for it and camp around it.
51 When the tabernacle must move, the Levites will take it down. When it must be set up, the Levites will set it up. Anyone else who comes near it must die.
52 The people of Israel must camp by their army groups. Each man must stay in his own camp under his own flag.

53 But the Levites must camp around the tabernacle of the Testimony. They must watch over it. Then no anger will come on the people of Israel. So the Levites are in charge of the tabernacle of the Testimony."
54 So the people of Israel did everything just as the LORD had told Moses.

title: Numbers 2
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Numbers
paragraphs: 1 Then the LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron.
2 "The people of Israel must camp around the Tent of Meeting. Each man must stay away from it. Each man must camp under his flag, with his family flag."
3 On the east side, toward the sunrise, the groups of Judah will camp under their flag.
4 The leader of the sons of Judah is Nahshon son of Amminadab. His group has 74,600 men.
5 The tribe of Issachar will camp next to Judah. Their leader is Nethanel son of Zuar.
6 His group has 54,400 men.
7 Next will be the tribe of Zebulun. Their leader is Eliab son of Helon.
8 His group has 57,400 men.
9 The camp of Judah has 186,400 men in total. They will march out first.
10 On the south side, the groups of Reuben will camp under their flag.
11 The leader of the sons of Reuben is Elizur son of Shedeur. His group has 46,500 men.
12 The tribe of Simeon will camp next to Reuben. Their leader is Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai.
13 His group has 59,300 men.
14 Next will be the tribe of Gad. Their leader is Eliasaph son of Deuel.
15 His group has 45,650 men.
16 The camp of Reuben has 151,450 men in total. They will march out second.

17 Then the Tent of Meeting will move with the camp of the Levites. It will go in the middle of the camp. The tribes will march out in the order they camped. Each man will stay in his place under his flag.
18 On the west side, the groups of Ephraim will camp under their flag.
19 The leader of the sons of Ephraim is Elishama son of Ammihud. His group has 40,500 men.
20 The tribe of Manasseh will be next to Ephraim. Their leader is Gamaliel son of Pedahzur.
21 His group has 32,200 men.
22 Next will be the tribe of Benjamin. Their leader is Abidan son of Gideoni.
23 His group has 35,400 men.
24 The camp of Ephraim has 108,100 men in total. They will march out third.
25 On the north side, the groups of Dan will camp under their flag.
26 The leader of the sons of Dan is Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai. His group has 62,700 men.
27 The tribe of Asher will camp next to Dan. Their leader is Pagiel son of Ocran.
28 His group has 41,500 men.
29 Next will be the tribe of Naphtali. Their leader is Ahira son of Enan.
30 His group has 53,400 men.
31 The camp of Dan has 157,600 men in total. They will march out last, under their flags."
32 These are the people of Israel, counted by their families. The total of all the men in the camps was 603,550.
33 But the Levites were not counted with the other Israelites. The LORD had told Moses not to count them.
34 So the people of Israel did everything the LORD told Moses. They camped under their flags in this way. They also marched out in the same way, each man with his family group and his family.

title: Numbers 3
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Numbers
paragraphs: 1 This is the story of Aaron and Moses. The LORD spoke with Moses on Mount Sinai.
2 These are the names of Aaron's sons: Nadab the firstborn, then Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
3 These were Aaron's sons. They were anointed priests, set apart to serve as priests.

4 But Nadab and Abihu died in front of the LORD. They offered fire that the LORD did not allow. This happened in the Desert of Sinai. They had no sons. So only Eleazar and Ithamar served as priests while their father Aaron lived.
5 Then the LORD said to Moses,
6 "Bring the tribe of Levi to Aaron the priest. They will help him.
7 They will do duties for him and for the whole community. They will work at the Tent of Meeting and serve the tabernacle.
8 They will take care of all the items of the Tent of Meeting. They will do duties for the Israelites and serve the tabernacle.
9 Give the Levites to Aaron and his sons. They belong only to him from among the Israelites.
10 Choose Aaron and his sons to do the duties of the priesthood. Any other person who comes near the tabernacle must die."
11 Again the LORD spoke to Moses. He said,
12 "Look, I have taken the Levites from the children of Israel. I took them instead of every firstborn Israelite from birth. The Levites belong to me.
13 All the firstborn are mine. On the day I killed every firstborn in Egypt, I set apart for myself all the firstborn in Israel. This was true for both man and animal. They are mine. I am the LORD."
14 Then the LORD spoke to Moses in the Desert of Sinai. He said,
15 "Count the Levites by their families and groups. Count every male one month old or more."
16 So Moses counted them as the LORD told him.
17 These were the sons of Levi by name: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.
18 These were the names of the sons of Gershon by their groups: Libni and Shimei.
19 The sons of Kohath by their groups were Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel.
20 The sons of Merari by their groups were Mahli and Mushi. These were the groups of the Levites by their families.
21 From Gershon came the Libnite group and the Shimeite group. These were the Gershonite groups.
22 The number of all the males one month old or more was 7,500.
23 The Gershonite groups were to camp on the west, behind the tabernacle.
24 The leader of the Gershonite families was Eliasaph son of Lael.
25 At the Tent of Meeting, the Gershonites cared for the tabernacle and tent. They cared for its covering and the curtain at the entrance,
26 the curtains of the courtyard, the curtain at the entrance to the courtyard around the tabernacle and altar, and the ropes. They did all the service for these items.
27 From Kohath came the groups of the Amramites, the Izharites, the Hebronites, and the Uzzielites. These were the groups of the Kohathites.
28 The number of all the males one month old or more was 8,600. They were in charge of the duties of the holy place.
29 The groups of the Kohathites were to camp on the south side of the tabernacle.
30 The leader of the Kohathite families was Elizaphan son of Uzziel.
31 They cared for the ark, the table, the lampstand, the altars, and the items of the holy place used with them. They also cared for the curtain. They did all the service for these items.
32 The chief of the Levite leaders was Eleazar son of Aaron the priest. He was in charge of those who did the duties of the holy place.
33 From Merari came the groups of the Mahlites and Mushites. These were the Merarite groups.
34 The number of all the males one month old or more was 6,200.
35 The leader of the Merarite families was Zuriel son of Abihail. They were to camp on the north side of the tabernacle.
36 The sons of Merari cared for the frames of the tabernacle, the crossbars, posts, bases, and all its equipment. They did all the service for these items.
37 They also cared for the posts of the courtyard around the tabernacle, with their bases, tent pegs, and ropes.

38 Moses, Aaron, and Aaron's sons were to camp east of the tabernacle, toward the sunrise. They camped in front of the Tent of Meeting. They did the duties of the holy place for the Israelites. Any other person who came near the holy place had to die.
39 Moses and Aaron counted all the Levites by their groups at the LORD's command. They counted all the males one month old or more. The total was 22,000.
40 Then the LORD said to Moses, "Count every firstborn male of the Israelites one month old or more. Write down their names.
41 Take the Levites for me — I am the LORD — instead of all the firstborn of Israel. Also take the animals of the Levites instead of all the firstborn animals of the Israelites."
42 So Moses counted all the firstborn of the Israelites, as the LORD told him.
43 The total number of firstborn males one month old or more, listed by name, was 22,273.
44 Again the LORD spoke to Moses. He said,
45 "Take the Levites instead of all the firstborn of Israel. Take the animals of the Levites instead of their animals. The Levites belong to me. I am the LORD.
46 There are 273 firstborn Israelites more than the Levites. To buy them back,
47 collect five shekels for each one. Use the sanctuary shekel of twenty gerahs.
48 Give the money to Aaron and his sons. It is the payment to buy back the extra Israelites."
49 So Moses collected the payment money. He collected it from those who were more than the Levites could redeem.
50 He collected the money from the firstborn of the Israelites. The amount was 1,365 shekels, by the sanctuary shekel.
51 Moses gave the payment money to Aaron and his sons. He did this just as the LORD had told him.

title: Numbers 4
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Numbers
paragraphs: 1 Then the LORD said to Moses and Aaron,
2 "Count the Kohathites among the Levites by their family groups and households.
3 Count men from thirty to fifty years old. Count everyone who is ready to serve at the Tent of Meeting.
4 The Kohathites will care for the most holy objects at the Tent of Meeting.
5 When the camp moves, Aaron and his sons must go in. They will take down the inner curtain and cover the ark of the Testimony with it.
6 Then they will put a fine leather covering over it. They will spread a solid blue cloth on top and put in its poles.
7 Over the table of the Presence they will spread a blue cloth. They will place the plates, cups, bowls, and pitchers for the drink offering on it. The regular bread offering must stay on it.
8 They will spread a red cloth over them. Then they will cover them with fine leather and put in the poles.
9 They will take a blue cloth. They will cover the lampstand with its lamps, tools for the lamps, and jars of oil for it.
10 Then they will wrap it and all its tools in fine leather. They will put it on the carrying frame.
11 Over the gold altar they will spread a blue cloth. They will cover it with fine leather and put in the poles.
12 They will take all the tools used in the holy place. They will place them in a blue cloth, cover them with fine leather, and put them on the carrying frame.
13 Then they will take the ashes from the bronze altar. They will spread a purple cloth over it.
14 They will place on it all the tools used there: the firepans, meat forks, shovels, and bowls — all the equipment of the altar. They will spread fine leather over it and put in the poles.

15 When Aaron and his sons finish covering the holy objects and all their equipment, the Kohathites will come to carry them. But they must not touch the holy objects, or they will die. These are the carrying duties of the Kohathites for the Tent of Meeting.

16 Eleazar son of Aaron the priest will care for the oil for the light. He will care for the sweet-smelling incense, the daily grain offering, and the special oil for anointing. He has care of the whole tabernacle and everything in it, including the holy objects and tools."
17 Then the LORD said to Moses and Aaron,
18 "Do not let the Kohathite family groups be lost from the Levites.
19 Do this so they may live and not die when they come near the most holy objects. Aaron and his sons will go in and give each man his task and load.
20 But the Kohathites must not go in and look at the holy objects, even for a moment, or they will die."
21 And the LORD said to Moses,
22 "Count the Gershonites too, by their households and family groups.
23 Count men from thirty to fifty years old. Count everyone who comes to serve at the Tent of Meeting.
24 This is the work of the Gershonite family groups for service and carrying:
25 They will carry the curtains of the tabernacle. They will carry the Tent of Meeting with its fine leather covering and the curtains for the entrance.

26 They will carry the curtains of the courtyard. They will carry the curtains for the gate of the courtyard around the tabernacle and altar. They will also carry their ropes and all the equipment for their service. The Gershonites will do all this work.
27 All the work of the Gershonites must be done under Aaron and his sons. This includes their carrying duties and other work. You will give them all that they must carry.
28 This is the work of the Gershonite family groups at the Tent of Meeting. Ithamar son of Aaron the priest will lead them.
29 Now count the sons of Merari by their family groups and households.
30 Count men from thirty to fifty years old. Count everyone who comes to serve at the Tent of Meeting.
31 This is their duty at the Tent of Meeting: to carry the frames of the tabernacle with its crossbars, posts, and bases,

32 and the posts of the courtyard around it with their bases, tent pegs, and ropes. They will carry all their equipment and everything used with them. You will assign by name the items each man must carry.
33 This is the work of the Merarite family groups at the Tent of Meeting. Ithamar son of Aaron the priest will lead them."
34 So Moses, Aaron, and the leaders of the community counted the Kohathites by their family groups and households.
35 They counted everyone from thirty to fifty years old who came to serve at the Tent of Meeting.
36 Those counted by their family groups totaled 2,750.
37 These were counted from the Kohathite family groups — everyone who could serve at the Tent of Meeting. Moses and Aaron counted them as the LORD told Moses.
38 Then the Gershonites were counted by their family groups and households.
39 They counted everyone from thirty to fifty years old who came to serve at the Tent of Meeting.
40 Those counted by their family groups and households totaled 2,630.
41 These were counted from the Gershonite family groups who served at the Tent of Meeting. Moses and Aaron counted them as the LORD told them.
42 And the Merarites were counted by their family groups and households.
43 They counted everyone from thirty to fifty years old who came to serve at the Tent of Meeting.
44 The men counted by their family groups totaled 3,200.
45 These were counted from the Merarite family groups. Moses and Aaron counted them as the LORD told Moses.
46 So Moses, Aaron, and the leaders of Israel counted all the Levites by their family groups and households.
47 They counted men from thirty to fifty years old who came to serve and carry at the Tent of Meeting.
48 The total number of men was 8,580.
49 As the LORD told them, they were counted through Moses. Each man was given his work and load, just as the LORD had commanded Moses.

title: Numbers 5
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Numbers
paragraphs: 1 Then the LORD said to Moses,
2 "Tell the Israelites to send away from the camp anyone with a skin disease. Send away anyone with a body discharge. Send away anyone made unclean by a dead body.
3 Send away both men and women. Send them outside the camp. Then they will not make their camp unclean, where I live with them."
4 So the Israelites did this. They sent such people outside the camp. They did just as the LORD had told Moses.
5 And the LORD said to Moses,
6 "Tell the Israelites this. A man or woman may break faith with the LORD. He or she may sin against someone else. That person is guilty.
7 That person must tell about the sin. He must pay back in full. He must add one fifth more. He must give it all to the person he hurt.
8 But the hurt person may have no relative. Then the payment belongs to the LORD. It must be given to the priest. He must also bring the ram for atonement, which brings him peace with God.
9 Every holy gift that the Israelites bring to the priest will belong to the priest.
10 Each man's holy gifts are his own. But what he gives to the priest belongs to the priest."
11 Then the LORD said to Moses,
12 "Speak to the Israelites and tell them this. A man's wife may go the wrong way. She may not be faithful to him.
13 She may sleep with another man, and her husband does not know. Her unclean act is hidden. No witness comes against her, and no one caught her.
14 A jealous feeling may come over her husband. He may doubt his wife who has become unclean. Or the jealous feeling may come, even if she is clean.

15 Then he must bring his wife to the priest. He must also bring an offering for her. The offering is one tenth of an ephah of barley flour. He must not pour oil on it or put frankincense on it. It is a grain offering for jealousy, a reminder of sin.
16 The priest must bring the wife forward. He must have her stand before the LORD.
17 Then he must take some holy water in a clay jar. He must put some dust from the tabernacle floor into the water.

18 The priest must have the woman stand before the LORD. He must loosen her hair. He must place the grain offering of memorial in her hands. This is the grain offering for jealousy. The priest must hold the bitter water that brings a curse.
19 He must put the woman under an oath. He must say, 'No other man has slept with you. You have not gone the wrong way as your husband's wife. Then this bitter water will not hurt you.
20 But you may have gone the wrong way as your husband's wife. You may have become unclean. You may have slept with another man.'

21 Then the priest will put the woman under the oath of the curse. He will say, 'May the LORD make you an open curse among your people. May he make your thigh waste away. May he make your belly swell.
22 May this water that brings a curse enter your stomach. May it cause your belly to swell. May it cause your thigh to waste away.' Then the woman must say, 'Amen, Amen.'
23 The priest must write these curses on a scroll. He must wash them off into the bitter water.
24 He must have the woman drink the bitter water that brings a curse. The water will enter her and cause bitter pain.
25 The priest must take the grain offering for jealousy from her hand. He must wave it before the LORD. He must bring it to the altar.
26 Then the priest must take a handful as a memorial portion. He must burn it on the altar. After that, he must have the woman drink the water.

27 When she drinks the water, this may happen. She may have become unclean and not been faithful to her husband. Then the water that brings a curse will enter her. It will cause bitter pain. Her belly will swell. Her thigh will waste away. She will be cursed among her people.
28 But the woman may not have become unclean. She may be clean. Then she will not be hurt. She will be able to have children.
29 This is the rule for jealousy. It is for when a wife goes the wrong way. It is for when she becomes unclean as her husband's wife.
30 It is also for when a jealous feeling comes over a husband. He may doubt his wife. He must have her stand before the LORD. The priest must apply this whole rule to her.
31 The husband will be free from guilt. But the woman will carry her own sin."

title: Numbers 6
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Numbers
paragraphs: 1 The LORD said to Moses,
2 "Speak to the Israelites. Tell them: If a man or woman makes a special promise, the Nazirite vow, to set himself apart for the LORD,
3 he must stay away from wine and strong drink. He must not drink vinegar made from wine or strong drink. He must not drink any grape juice or eat fresh grapes or raisins.
4 During all the days he is set apart, he must not eat anything from the grape plant — not even the seeds or skins.
5 During the whole time of his vow, no razor must touch his head. He must be holy until his time with the LORD is complete. He must let his hair grow long.
6 During all the days he is set apart for the LORD, he must not go near a dead body.
7 Even if his father, mother, brother, or sister dies, he must not make himself unclean. The sign of being set apart to his God is on his head.
8 During his whole time of being set apart, he is holy to the LORD.
9 If someone suddenly dies near him, his hair that was set apart becomes unclean. He must shave his head on the day he is made clean — the seventh day.
10 On the eighth day he must bring two doves or two young pigeons to the priest at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting.
11 The priest will offer one as a sin offering and the other as a burnt offering. This will cover his sin, because he was near a dead body. On that day he must set apart his head again.
12 He must start his time of being set apart again. He must bring a one-year-old male lamb as a guilt offering. The days before do not count, because his time was made unclean.
13 This is the law of the Nazirite when his time is complete. He must be brought to the entrance to the Tent of Meeting.

14 He must bring an offering to the LORD. He brings a one-year-old male lamb with no faults as a burnt offering. He brings a one-year-old female lamb with no faults as a sin offering. He brings a ram with no faults as a peace offering.
15 He also brings grain offerings and drink offerings. He brings a basket of flat cakes made from fine flour mixed with oil. He brings flat wafers with oil on them. None of them have yeast.
16 The priest will bring all these before the LORD. He will make the sin offering and the burnt offering.
17 He will also offer the ram as a peace offering to the LORD, with the basket of bread that has no yeast. The priest will also offer the grain offering and drink offering.
18 Then at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting, the Nazirite must shave his head that was set apart. He takes the hair and puts it on the fire under the peace offering.
19 The priest takes the cooked shoulder from the ram. He takes one flat cake from the basket and one flat wafer. He puts them into the hands of the Nazirite, who has just shaved his hair.
20 The priest will wave them as a wave offering before the LORD. This is a holy share for the priest. It is added to the chest and the leg that were waved. After this, the Nazirite may drink wine.
21 This is the law of the Nazirite who makes a vow. He brings his offering to the LORD for being set apart. He may bring more if he can. He must do whatever he promised, by the law of his vow."
22 The LORD said to Moses,
23 "Tell Aaron and his sons: This is how you must bless the Israelites. Say to them:
24 'The LORD bless you and keep you;
25 the LORD make his face shine on you and be gracious to you;
26 the LORD turn his face toward you and give you peace.'
27 So they will put my name on the Israelites, and I will bless them."

title: Numbers 7
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Numbers
paragraphs: 1 Moses finished setting up the tabernacle one day. He poured oil on it and set it apart. He did the same for all its parts. He did the same for the altar and all its tools.
2 Then the leaders of Israel brought an offering. They were the heads of their families. These men were the tribal leaders. They had counted the people.
3 They brought their offering to the LORD. They brought six covered carts and twelve oxen. Each leader gave one ox. Every two leaders gave one cart. They brought them to the tabernacle.
4 The LORD said to Moses,
5 "Accept these gifts from them. Use them for the work of the Tent of Meeting. Give them to the Levites. Give each man what his service needs."
6 So Moses took the carts and the oxen. He gave them to the Levites.
7 He gave the Gershonites two carts and four oxen. Their service needed this much.
8 He gave the Merarites four carts and eight oxen. Their service needed this much. Ithamar son of Aaron the priest led this work.
9 But Moses did not give any to the Kohathites. They had to carry the holy things on their shoulders.
10 Then the leaders brought offerings for the dedication of the altar. They brought them when the altar was set apart with oil. They put them before the altar.
11 The LORD said to Moses, "Each day one leader will bring his offering. This is for the dedication of the altar."
12 On the first day, Nahshon son of Amminadab came near. He was from the tribe of Judah.

13 His offering was one silver plate. It weighed 130 shekels. He also brought one silver bowl. It weighed 70 shekels. Both used the holy shekel. Both were full of fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering.
14 He brought one gold dish. It weighed 10 shekels. It was full of incense.
15 He brought one young bull, one ram, and one male lamb one year old. These were for a burnt offering.
16 He brought one male goat for a sin offering.
17 For a peace offering he brought two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs one year old. This was the offering of Nahshon son of Amminadab.
18 On the second day, Nethanel son of Zuar came near. He was the leader of Issachar.

19 He brought one silver plate. It weighed 130 shekels. He also brought one silver bowl. It weighed 70 shekels. Both used the holy shekel. Both were full of fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering.
20 He brought one gold dish. It weighed 10 shekels. It was full of incense.
21 He brought one young bull, one ram, and one male lamb one year old. These were for a burnt offering.
22 He brought one male goat for a sin offering.
23 For a peace offering he brought two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs one year old. This was the offering of Nethanel son of Zuar.
24 On the third day, Eliab son of Helon came near. He was the leader of the Zebulunites.

25 His offering was one silver plate. It weighed 130 shekels. He also brought one silver bowl. It weighed 70 shekels. Both used the holy shekel. Both were full of fine flour mixed with oil for a grain offering.
26 He brought one gold dish. It weighed 10 shekels. It was full of incense.
27 He brought one young bull, one ram, and one male lamb one year old. These were for a burnt offering.
28 He brought one male goat for a sin offering.
29 For a peace offering he brought two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs one year old. This was the offering of Eliab son of Helon.
30 On the fourth day, Elizur son of Shedeur came near. He was the leader of the Reubenites.

title: Numbers 8
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Numbers
paragraphs: 1 Then the LORD said to Moses,
2 "Speak to Aaron. Tell him: 'When you set up the seven lamps, they must shine on the area in front of the lampstand.'"
3 Aaron did so. He set up the lamps facing the front of the lampstand, just as the LORD had told Moses.
4 This is how they made the lampstand. They made it from gold beaten into shape, from its base to its flowers. They made it just like the pattern the LORD had shown Moses.
5 Again the LORD spoke to Moses. He said,
6 "Take the Levites from among the Israelites. Make them clean before God.
7 This is what you must do to clean them. Sprinkle them with the water that makes clean. Have them shave their whole bodies. Have them wash their clothes. In this way they will be clean.
8 Then have them take a young bull. With it, take a grain offering of fine flour mixed with oil. Take a second young bull for a sin offering.
9 Bring the Levites in front of the Tent of Meeting. Gather all the people of Israel.
10 Present the Levites before the LORD. Have the Israelites lay their hands on them.
11 Aaron must present the Levites before the LORD as a wave offering from the Israelites. Then they can do the work of the LORD.
12 The Levites must lay their hands on the heads of the bulls. Offer one bull to the LORD as a sin offering. Offer the other as a burnt offering. This will make things right with God for the Levites.
13 Have the Levites stand before Aaron and his sons. Then present them to the LORD as a wave offering.
14 In this way, you will set the Levites apart from the rest of the Israelites. The Levites will belong to Me.
15 After you have cleaned them and presented them as a wave offering, they may come to serve at the Tent of Meeting.
16 The Levites have been given to Me completely from among the Israelites. I have taken them for Myself. I took them instead of every firstborn son in Israel.
17 Every firstborn male in Israel is Mine — both people and animals. I set them apart for Myself on the day I killed all the firstborn in Egypt.
18 But I have taken the Levites instead of all the firstborn sons of Israel.

19 I have given the Levites as a gift to Aaron and his sons. They will do the service for the Israelites at the Tent of Meeting. They will make things right with God for them. In this way, no sickness will come on the Israelites when they come near the holy place."
20 So Moses, Aaron, and all the people of Israel did this with the Levites. They did everything the LORD had told Moses to do.
21 The Levites made themselves clean and washed their clothes. Aaron presented them as a wave offering before the LORD. Aaron also made things right with God for them, to clean them.
22 After that, the Levites came to do their service at the Tent of Meeting. They served before Aaron and his sons. They did with the Levites just as the LORD had told Moses.
23 And the LORD said to Moses,
24 "This is the rule for the Levites. Men twenty-five years old or older must come and do the service in the work at the Tent of Meeting.
25 But at the age of fifty, they must stop doing the work. They will no longer serve.
26 After that, they may help their brothers with their duties at the Tent of Meeting. But they themselves must not do the work. This is how you will give jobs to the Levites."

title: Numbers 9
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Numbers
paragraphs: 1 It was the first month of the second year after Israel left Egypt. The LORD spoke to Moses in the Wilderness of Sinai.
2 "The Israelites must keep the Passover at its set time.
3 Keep it at its set time, at sunset on the fourteenth day of this month. Follow all its rules."
4 So Moses told the Israelites to keep the Passover.
5 They kept it in the Wilderness of Sinai, at sunset on the fourteenth day. It was the first month. The Israelites did everything just as the LORD had told Moses.
6 But some men were unclean from touching a dead body. So they could not keep the Passover that day. They came before Moses and Aaron that same day.
7 They said to Moses, "We are unclean from touching a dead body. But why should we miss the LORD's offering at its set time? Why not share it with the other Israelites?"
8 Moses replied, "Wait here until I find out what the LORD commands about you."
9 Then the LORD said to Moses,

10 "Tell the Israelites: 'Maybe a man touches a dead body and becomes unclean. Or maybe he is away on a journey. This may be you or your children's children. He may still keep the Passover to the LORD.
11 Such people must keep it at sunset on the fourteenth day of the second month. They must eat the lamb with unleavened bread and bitter herbs.
12 They must not leave any of it until morning. They must not break any of its bones. They must keep the Passover by all its rules.

13 But say a man is clean and is not on a journey. If he does not keep the Passover, he must be cut off from his people. He did not bring the LORD's offering at its set time. That man will carry the weight of his sin.
14 Say a foreigner lives among you and wants to keep the Passover to the LORD. He must do so by the Passover rules. The same rule applies to the foreigner and to the native of the land.'"
15 On the day the tabernacle was set up, the cloud covered it. This was the Tent of the Testimony. From evening until morning, the cloud looked like fire above the tabernacle.
16 It stayed that way all the time. The cloud covered the tabernacle by day. At night it looked like fire.
17 Whenever the cloud lifted from above the Tent, the Israelites moved on. Wherever the cloud settled, the Israelites camped.
18 At the LORD's command the Israelites moved on. At the LORD's command they camped. As long as the cloud stayed over the tabernacle, they stayed in camp.
19 Sometimes the cloud stayed over the tabernacle for many days. The Israelites obeyed the LORD's command. They did not move on.
20 Sometimes the cloud stayed over the tabernacle for only a few days. They camped at the LORD's command and moved on at the LORD's command.
21 Sometimes the cloud stayed only from evening until morning. When it lifted in the morning, they moved on. Whether by day or by night, when the cloud lifted, they moved on.
22 The cloud might stay for two days, a month, or longer. While it stayed over the tabernacle, the Israelites camped. They did not move on. But when it lifted, they moved on.
23 They camped at the LORD's command. They moved on at the LORD's command. They obeyed the LORD's command through Moses.

title: Numbers 10
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Numbers
paragraphs: 1 Then the LORD spoke to Moses.
2 "Make two trumpets from beaten silver. Use them to call the people and to start the camps moving.
3 When you blow both trumpets, all the people must gather. They must come to you at the Tent of Meeting.
4 But if you blow only one, the leaders must come to you. These are the heads of the clans of Israel.
5 When you blow short loud sounds, the camps on the east side must leave.
6 When you blow short sounds a second time, the camps on the south side must leave. The short sounds tell them to start moving.
7 To call the people together, blow long steady sounds, not short ones.
8 The sons of Aaron, the priests, must blow the trumpets. This will be a lasting rule for all your future families.
9 When you go to war in your land against an enemy who attacks you, blow short sounds on the trumpets. Then the LORD your God will remember you. He will save you from your enemies.

10 On your happy days, your special feast days, and the first day of each month, blow the trumpets. Blow them over your burnt offerings and fellowship offerings. They will be a reminder for you before your God. I am the LORD your God."
11 On the twentieth day of the second month of the second year, the cloud rose. It lifted from above the tabernacle of the Testimony.
12 So the Israelites left the Wilderness of Sinai. They traveled from place to place. The cloud stopped in the Wilderness of Paran.
13 This was the first time they set out by the LORD's command through Moses.
14 First, the groups of the camp of Judah set out under their flag. Nahshon son of Amminadab was their leader.
15 Nethanel son of Zuar led the group from the tribe of Issachar.
16 Eliab son of Helon led the group from the tribe of Zebulun.
17 Then the tabernacle was taken down. The Gershonites and the Merarites set out, carrying it.
18 Then the groups of the camp of Reuben set out under their flag. Elizur son of Shedeur was their leader.
19 Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai led the group from the tribe of Simeon.
20 Eliasaph son of Deuel led the group from the tribe of Gad.
21 Then the Kohathites set out, carrying the holy objects. The tabernacle had to be set up before they arrived.
22 Next, the groups of the camp of Ephraim set out under their flag. Elishama son of Ammihud was their leader.
23 Gamaliel son of Pedahzur led the group from the tribe of Manasseh.
24 Abidan son of Gideoni led the group from the tribe of Benjamin.
25 Finally, the groups of the camp of Dan set out under their flag. They were the last group at the back, guarding all the others. Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai was their leader.
26 Pagiel son of Ocran led the group from the tribe of Asher.
27 Ahira son of Enan led the group from the tribe of Naphtali.
28 This was the order of march. This is how the Israelite groups set out.

29 Then Moses spoke to Hobab. Hobab was the son of Reuel the Midianite, Moses' father-in-law. Moses said, "We are leaving for the place the LORD promised. He said, 'I will give it to you.' Come with us, and we will be good to you. The LORD has promised good things to Israel."
30 "I will not go," Hobab replied. "I am going back to my own land and my own people."
31 "Please do not leave us," Moses said. "You know where we should camp in the wilderness. You can be our eyes.
32 If you come with us, we will share with you whatever good things the LORD gives us."
33 So they set out from the mountain of the LORD on a three-day journey. The ark of the covenant of the LORD went ahead of them. For three days it searched for a resting place for them.
34 The cloud of the LORD was over them by day. It went with them when they left the camp.
35 Whenever the ark set out, Moses would say, "Rise up, O LORD! May Your enemies be scattered. May those who hate You run away from You."
36 And when the ark came to rest, he would say, "Return, O LORD, to the many thousands of Israel."

title: Numbers 11
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Numbers
paragraphs: 1 Soon the people began to complain about their troubles. The LORD heard them and became very angry. Fire from the LORD blazed among them and burned the edge of the camp.
2 The people cried out to Moses, and he prayed to the LORD. Then the fire died down.
3 So that place was called Taberah, because the fire of the LORD had burned among them.
4 The mixed crowd among them had a strong wish for other food. The Israelites wept again and said, "Who will give us meat to eat?
5 We remember the fish we ate freely in Egypt. We had cucumbers, melons, leeks, onions, and garlic.
6 But now we have no appetite. There is nothing to see but this manna!"
7 The manna looked like coriander seeds. It looked like tree sap.

8 The people walked around and gathered it. They ground it in a small mill or crushed it in a stone bowl. Then they boiled it in a pot or made cakes from it. It tasted like pastry baked with fine oil.
9 When the dew fell on the camp at night, the manna fell with it.
10 Moses heard the people weeping, family after family, at the doors of their tents. The LORD became very angry, and Moses was also upset.
11 So Moses asked the LORD, "Why have You brought this trouble on Your servant? Why have I not found favor in Your sight? You have put the burden of all these people on me.
12 Did I give birth to all these people? Did I bring them into the world? Why do You tell me, 'Carry them in your arms, as a nurse carries a baby,' to the land You promised their fathers?
13 Where can I get meat for all these people? They keep crying to me, 'Give us meat to eat!'
14 I cannot carry all these people by myself. The burden is too heavy for me.
15 If this is how You will treat me, please kill me right now. If I have found favor in Your eyes, do not let me see my own misery."

16 Then the LORD said to Moses, "Bring Me seventy of the elders of Israel. Choose men known to you as leaders and officers of the people. Bring them to the Tent of Meeting and let them stand there with you.

17 I will come down and speak with you there. I will take some of the Spirit that is on you and put it on them. They will help you carry the burden of the people. Then you will not have to carry it alone.

18 And say to the people: Make yourselves holy for tomorrow, and you will eat meat. You cried out in the hearing of the LORD. You said, 'Who will give us meat? We were better off in Egypt!' So the LORD will give you meat, and you will eat.
19 You will not eat it for just one or two days. You will not eat it for five or ten or twenty days,

20 but for a whole month. You will eat it until it comes out of your nose and makes you sick. You have rejected the LORD, who is among you. You cried out before Him, saying, 'Why did we ever leave Egypt?'"
21 But Moses said, "I am here among 600,000 men on foot. Yet You say, 'I will give them meat, and they will eat for a month.'
22 Could we kill all our flocks and herds for them? Would that be enough? Could we catch all the fish in the sea for them? Would that be enough?"
23 The LORD answered Moses, "Is the LORD's arm too short? Now you will see if My word comes true or not."
24 So Moses went out and told the people the words of the LORD. He gathered seventy of the elders of the people and had them stand around the tent.

25 Then the LORD came down in the cloud and spoke to him. He took some of the Spirit that was on Moses and placed it on the seventy elders. When the Spirit rested on them, they prophesied. But they never did so again.

26 Two men had stayed in the camp. One was named Eldad and the other Medad. The Spirit rested on them too. They were on the list, but they had not gone out to the tent. So they prophesied in the camp.
27 A young man ran and told Moses, "Eldad and Medad are prophesying in the camp."
28 Joshua son of Nun had served Moses since he was young. He spoke up and said, "Moses, my lord, stop them!"
29 But Moses replied, "Are you jealous for my sake? I wish that all the LORD's people were prophets. I wish the LORD would put His Spirit on them all!"
30 Then Moses returned to the camp with the elders of Israel.

31 Now a wind sent by the LORD came up. It drove quail in from the sea and brought them near the camp. The quail were about two cubits (about 1 meter) above the ground. They covered a day's journey in every direction around the camp.
32 The people stayed up all that day and night, and all the next day, gathering the quail. No one gathered less than ten homers (large baskets). They spread the quail out all around the camp.
33 But while the meat was still between their teeth, before they had chewed it, the LORD became very angry with the people. The LORD struck them with a terrible sickness.
34 So they called that place Kibroth-hattaavah, because there they buried the people who had wished for other food.
35 From Kibroth-hattaavah the people moved on to Hazeroth, where they stayed for some time.

title: Numbers 12
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Numbers
paragraphs: 1 Then Miriam and Aaron spoke against Moses. They spoke against him because of his Cushite wife.
2 They said, "Does the LORD speak only through Moses? Does He not also speak through us?" And the LORD heard this.
3 Now Moses was a very humble man. He was more humble than any man on the earth.
4 Suddenly the LORD spoke to Moses, Aaron, and Miriam. He said, "You three, come out to the Tent of Meeting." So the three went out.
5 The LORD came down in a tall cloud. He stood at the door of the Tent. He called Aaron and Miriam, and both stepped forward.
6 He said, "Hear my words now. If there is a prophet among you, I, the LORD, will show myself to him in a vision. I will speak to him in a dream.
7 But this is not so with my servant Moses. He is faithful in all my house.
8 I speak with him face to face. I speak clearly, not in hidden words. He sees the form of the LORD. Why then were you not afraid to speak against my servant Moses?"
9 So the anger of the LORD burned against them, and He went away.
10 As the cloud lifted from the Tent, Miriam suddenly became sick with leprosy, white as snow. Aaron turned to her and saw that she was sick.
11 He said to Moses, "My lord, please do not hold this sin against us. We have done it in a foolish way.
12 Please do not let her be like a dead baby. Its body is half rotten when it comes out of the mother."
13 So Moses cried out to the LORD. He said, "O God, please heal her!"

14 But the LORD answered Moses, "If her father had spit in her face, would she not have shame for seven days? Let her stay shut outside the camp for seven days. After that, you may bring her back in."
15 So Miriam was shut outside the camp for seven days. The people did not move on until she came back in.
16 After that, the people set out from Hazeroth. They camped in the Wilderness of Paran.

title: Numbers 13
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Numbers
paragraphs: 1 The LORD said to Moses,
2 "Send out men to explore the land of Canaan. I am giving this land to the Israelites. Send one leader from each tribe."
3 So Moses sent them from the Wilderness of Paran, just as the LORD said. All of them were leaders of the Israelites.
4 These were their names: From the tribe of Reuben, Shammua son of Zaccur;
5 from the tribe of Simeon, Shaphat son of Hori;
6 from the tribe of Judah, Caleb son of Jephunneh;
7 from the tribe of Issachar, Igal son of Joseph;
8 from the tribe of Ephraim, Hoshea son of Nun;
9 from the tribe of Benjamin, Palti son of Raphu;
10 from the tribe of Zebulun, Gaddiel son of Sodi;
11 from the tribe of Manasseh (a tribe of Joseph), Gaddi son of Susi;
12 from the tribe of Dan, Ammiel son of Gemalli;
13 from the tribe of Asher, Sethur son of Michael;
14 from the tribe of Naphtali, Nahbi son of Vophsi;
15 and from the tribe of Gad, Geuel son of Machi.
16 These were the men Moses sent to explore the land. Moses gave Hoshea son of Nun the name Joshua.
17 Moses sent them to explore the land of Canaan. He told them, "Go up through the Negev and into the hill country.
18 See what the land is like. Find out if the people are strong or weak, few or many.
19 Is the land where they live good or bad? Are their cities open camps or do they have strong walls?
20 Is the soil rich or poor? Are there trees in it or not? Be brave, and bring back some fruit of the land." It was the season for the first ripe grapes.
21 So they went up and explored the land. They went from the Wilderness of Zin as far as Rehob, near Lebo-hamath.
22 They went up through the Negev and came to Hebron. Ahiman, Sheshai, and Talmai, the sons of Anak, lived there. Hebron was built seven years before Zoan in Egypt.
23 They came to the Valley of Eshcol. There they cut down a branch with one cluster of grapes. Two men carried it on a pole between them. They also took some pomegranates and figs.
24 That place was called the Valley of Eshcol because of the grapes the Israelites cut there.
25 After forty days the men came back from exploring the land.
26 They returned to Moses, Aaron, and all the people of Israel. They came to the Wilderness of Paran at Kadesh. They brought back a report and showed the fruit of the land.
27 They told Moses, "We went into the land where you sent us. It truly flows with milk and honey. Here is some of its fruit!
28 But the people who live there are strong. The cities are large and have strong walls. We even saw the sons of Anak there.
29 The Amalekites live in the Negev. The Hittites, Jebusites, and Amorites live in the hill country. The Canaanites live by the sea and along the Jordan."
30 Then Caleb quieted the people before Moses. He said, "We should go up and take possession of the land, for we can certainly do it."
31 But the men who had gone with him said, "We cannot go up against the people. They are stronger than we are!"
32 So they spread a bad report about the land they had explored. They said, "The land we explored eats up the people who live there. All the people we saw are very tall.
33 We even saw the Nephilim there — the sons of Anak come from the Nephilim! We seemed like grasshoppers in our own eyes, and we looked the same to them."

title: Numbers 14
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Numbers
paragraphs: 1 Then all the people cried out loudly, and they wailed that night.
2 All the Israelites complained against Moses and Aaron. The people said to them,

3 "If only we had died in Egypt! If only we had died in this wilderness! Why is the LORD bringing us to this land to die by the sword? Our wives and children will be taken as prizes. Should we not go back to Egypt?"
4 And they said to one another, "Let us choose another leader and return to Egypt."
5 Then Moses and Aaron fell facedown before all the people of Israel.
6 Joshua son of Nun and Caleb son of Jephunneh had explored the land. They tore their clothes
7 They spoke to all the people. They said, "The land we passed through and explored is very good.
8 If the LORD is pleased with us, he will lead us into that land. It flows with milk and honey, and he will give it to us.
9 Only do not rebel against the LORD. And do not be afraid of the people of the land. They will be like bread for us. Their protection is gone, and the LORD is with us. Do not be afraid of them!"
10 But all the people talked of stoning Joshua and Caleb. Then the glory of the LORD appeared to all the Israelites. It appeared at the Tent of Meeting.
11 The LORD said to Moses, "How long will these people treat me with no respect? How long will they refuse to believe in me? I have done so many signs among them.
12 I will strike them with a plague and destroy them. Then I will make you into a nation greater and stronger than they are."
13 But Moses said to the LORD, "The Egyptians will hear about it. By your strength you brought these people out from among them.

14 And they will tell the people who live in this land. They have already heard that you, LORD, are with these people. They have heard that you have been seen face to face. They have heard that your cloud stands over them. You go before them in a pillar of cloud by day. You go in a pillar of fire by night.
15 If you kill these people all at once, the nations will hear of it. They will say,
16 'The LORD was not able to bring these people into the land he promised. So he killed them in the wilderness.'
17 So now, I pray, let the power of my Lord be shown great. You have said:

18 'The LORD is slow to anger and full of love. He forgives sin and rebellion. Yet he will not leave the guilty without punishment. He brings the sin of fathers on their children to the third and fourth generation.'
19 In accordance with your great love, forgive the sin of these people. Forgive them just as you have pardoned them from Egypt until now."
20 The LORD replied, "I have forgiven them, as you asked.
21 Yet as surely as I live, the glory of the LORD fills the whole earth.
22 not one of those who saw my glory will see it. They saw the signs I performed in Egypt and in the wilderness. Yet they have tested me these ten times and disobeyed me.
23 Not one of them will ever see the land. I promised it on oath to their fathers. No one who treated me with no respect will see it.
24 But my servant Caleb has a different spirit. He has followed me with his whole heart. I will bring him into the land he entered, and his descendants will receive it.
25 Now the Amalekites and Canaanites live in the valleys. So turn back tomorrow. Head into the wilderness along the road to the Red Sea."
26 Then the LORD said to Moses and Aaron,
27 "How long will this wicked people complain against me? I have heard the complaints the Israelites make against me.
28 So tell them this declaration of the LORD. 'As surely as I live, I will do to you what I heard you say.
29 Your bodies will fall in this wilderness. You were counted in the census — everyone twenty years old or older. You will die here, because you complained against me.
30 None of you will enter the land I promised to settle you in. Only Caleb son of Jephunneh and Joshua son of Nun will enter.
31 You said your children would be taken as prizes. But I will bring them into the land you rejected. They will enjoy it.
32 As for you, your bodies will fall in this wilderness.
33 Your children will be shepherds in the wilderness for forty years. They will suffer for your unfaithfulness. Their suffering will last until the last of your bodies lies in the wilderness.
34 You explored the land for forty days. So you will bear your guilt forty years — one year for each day. Then you will know what it is like to have me against you.
35 I, the LORD, have spoken. I will surely do these things to this wicked people. They have joined together against me. They will meet their end in this wilderness. There they will die.'"
36 So the men whom Moses had sent to explore the land returned. They gave a bad report about the land. They made all the people complain against him.
37 The LORD struck down those men with a plague. They were the ones who gave the bad report.
38 Of the men who had explored the land, only two stayed alive. They were Joshua son of Nun and Caleb son of Jephunneh.
39 Moses told these words to all the Israelites, and the people mourned deeply.
40 Early the next morning they got up. They went up toward the top of the hill country. They said, "We have sinned. But we will go to the place the LORD has promised."
41 But Moses said, "Why are you breaking the command of the LORD? This will not succeed!
42 Do not go up. Your enemies will strike you down, because the LORD is not with you.
43 The Amalekites and Canaanites are there before you. You will fall by the sword. You have turned away from the LORD, so he will not be with you."
44 But they dared to go up to the top of the hill country. Yet Moses did not move from the camp. The ark of the covenant of the LORD did not move either.
45 Then the Amalekites and Canaanites who lived in that hill country came down. They attacked them and chased them all the way to Hormah.

title: Numbers 15
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Numbers
paragraphs: 1 Then the LORD said to Moses,
2 "Speak to the people of Israel and tell them: You will enter the land I am giving you as your home.

3 When you give an offering burned with fire to the LORD from your cattle or sheep, it must make a sweet smell that pleases Him. It may be a burnt offering or a sacrifice. It may be for a special promise, a free gift, or a set feast day.
4 Then the person who brings the offering must also bring a grain offering. It must be a tenth of an ephah of fine flour mixed with a quarter hin of olive oil.
5 With each lamb for a burnt offering or sacrifice, prepare a quarter hin of wine as a drink offering.
6 With a ram, prepare a grain offering of two-tenths of an ephah of fine flour. Mix it with a third of a hin of olive oil.
7 Also bring a third of a hin of wine as a drink offering. It will make a sweet smell that pleases the LORD.
8 You may prepare a young bull as a burnt offering or sacrifice. It may be to keep a promise or as a peace offering to the LORD.
9 Then bring with the bull a grain offering of three-tenths of an ephah of fine flour. Mix it with half a hin of olive oil.
10 Also bring half a hin of wine as a drink offering. It is an offering burned with fire, a sweet smell that pleases the LORD.
11 Do this for each bull, ram, lamb, or goat.
12 Prepare each one this way, no matter how many you bring.
13 Everyone born in Israel must prepare these things this way. They will bring an offering burned with fire, a sweet smell that pleases the LORD.
14 In the years to come, a foreigner may live with you. He may want to bring an offering burned with fire, a sweet smell that pleases the LORD. He must do it the same way you do.
15 The whole group must have the same rule for you and for the foreigner. This is a rule that lasts for all the years to come. You and the foreigner are the same before the LORD.
16 The same law and the same rule will be for you and for the foreigner who lives with you."
17 Then the LORD said to Moses,
18 "Speak to the people of Israel and tell them: You will enter the land I am bringing you to.
19 When you eat the food of the land, lift up an offering to the LORD.
20 From the first of your dough, lift up a cake as a gift. Offer it just like an offering from the threshing floor.
21 In all the years to come, give the LORD an offering from the first of your dough.
22 Now you may sin by mistake. You may fail to obey all these commands the LORD spoke to Moses.
23 These are all the commands the LORD gave you through Moses, from the first day He gave them and on through the years to come.

24 It may happen by mistake, without the whole group knowing. Then the whole group must prepare one young bull as a burnt offering. It will make a sweet smell that pleases the LORD. They must also bring its grain offering and drink offering as the rule says. And they must bring one male goat as a sin offering.
25 The priest will cover the sin of the whole group of Israel. Then they will be forgiven. The sin was by mistake. They brought to the LORD an offering burned with fire and a sin offering for it.
26 Then the whole group of Israel and the foreigners who live with them will be forgiven. The sin happened to all the people by mistake.
27 One person may also sin by mistake. He must bring a female goat one year old as a sin offering.
28 The priest will cover the sin before the LORD for the person who sinned by mistake. When his sin is covered, he will be forgiven.
29 You will have the same law for everyone who sins by mistake. It is the same for a person born in Israel and for a foreigner who lives with you.
30 But the person who sins on purpose insults the LORD. This is true for a native or a foreigner. That person must be cut off from his people.
31 He will surely be cut off. He has hated the word of the LORD and broken His command. His guilt stays on him."
32 While the people of Israel were in the desert, they found a man gathering wood on the Sabbath day.
33 The people who found him brought him to Moses, Aaron, and the whole group.
34 They had not been told what to do with him. So they kept him under guard.
35 Then the LORD said to Moses, "The man must die. The whole group must throw stones at him outside the camp."
36 So the whole group took the man outside the camp. They threw stones at him until he died, as the LORD had commanded Moses.
37 Later the LORD said to Moses,
38 "Speak to the people of Israel and tell them this. In all the years to come, they must make tassels for the corners of their clothes. Each tassel must have a blue cord on it.
39 When you look at these tassels, you will remember all the commands of the LORD. Then you will obey them. You will not turn away from me by following your own heart and your own eyes.
40 You will remember and obey all my commands. You will be set apart for your God.
41 I am the LORD your God. I brought you out of the land of Egypt to be your God. I am the LORD your God."

title: Numbers 16
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Numbers
paragraphs: 1 Korah was the son of Izhar. Izhar was the son of Kohath, son of Levi. Korah took some Reubenites with him. They were Dathan and Abiram, sons of Eliab, and On son of Peleth.
2 They started a rebellion against Moses. With them were 250 men of Israel. They were well-known leaders of the people.
3 They came together against Moses and Aaron. They said, "You have taken too much power! All the people are holy. The LORD is with them. Why do you lift yourselves above the LORD's people?"
4 When Moses heard this, he fell to the ground with his face down.
5 Then he said to Korah and his followers, "Tomorrow morning the LORD will show who belongs to Him. He will show who is holy. He will bring that person near to Himself."
6 "You, Korah, and all your followers, do this: Take censers.
7 Tomorrow put fire and incense in them before the LORD. The man the LORD chooses will be the holy one. You sons of Levi have taken too much power!"
8 Moses also said to Korah, "Now listen, you sons of Levi!
9 The God of Israel has set you apart from the other people. He brought you near to do the work at the LORD's tabernacle. You stand before the people to serve them. Is this not enough?
10 He has brought you and your fellow Levites near to Him. But now you also want to be priests.
11 So you and your followers have joined together against the LORD! Why do you complain about Aaron?"
12 Then Moses called for Dathan and Abiram, the sons of Eliab. But they said, "We will not come!
13 You brought us out of a land full of milk and honey. You brought us to die in the wilderness. Is that not enough? Now do you want to rule over us too?
14 You have not brought us to a land full of milk and honey. You have not given us fields and vineyards. Will you trick these men? No, we will not come!"
15 Then Moses became very angry. He said to the LORD, "Do not accept their offering. I have not taken one donkey from them. I have not hurt one of them."
16 Moses said to Korah, "You and all your followers must come before the LORD tomorrow. You, they, and Aaron must come.
17 Each man must take his censer. Put incense in it. Bring it before the LORD—250 censers. You and Aaron must bring your censers too."
18 So each man took his censer. He put fire and incense in it. They stood with Moses and Aaron at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting.
19 Korah had gathered all his people against them at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting. Then the glory of the LORD appeared to all the people.
20 The LORD said to Moses and Aaron,
21 "Move away from these people. I will destroy them right away."
22 But Moses and Aaron fell to the ground with their faces down. They said, "O God, You give life to all people. One man sins. Will You be angry with everyone?"
23 Then the LORD said to Moses,
24 "Tell the people to move away from the tents of Korah, Dathan, and Abiram."
25 So Moses got up. He went to Dathan and Abiram. The elders of Israel followed him.
26 He warned the people, "Move away now from the tents of these evil men. Do not touch anything that belongs to them. If you do, you will be swept away with their sins."
27 So they moved away from the tents of Korah, Dathan, and Abiram. Dathan and Abiram had come out. They stood at the entrances to their tents with their wives, children, and babies.
28 Then Moses said, "This is how you will know the LORD sent me. He sent me to do all these things. I did not plan this on my own.
29 If these men die a normal death, like all people, then the LORD has not sent me.

30 But the LORD may do something never seen before. The earth may open its mouth. It may swallow them and all they own. They may go down alive into Sheol, the place of the dead. Then you will know these men have shown no respect for the LORD."
31 As soon as Moses finished speaking, the ground under them split open.
32 The earth opened its mouth. It swallowed them and their families. It swallowed all of Korah's men and everything they owned.
33 They went down alive into Sheol with all they had. The earth closed over them. They were gone from among the people.
34 All the Israelites near them ran away when they heard their cries. They said, "The earth may swallow us too!"
35 Then fire came from the LORD. It burned up the 250 men who were offering the incense.
36 Then the LORD said to Moses,
37 "Tell Eleazar son of Aaron the priest to take the censers from the fire. Tell him to scatter the hot coals far away. The censers are holy.
38 These men sinned and lost their lives. But their censers are holy now. They were brought before the LORD. Hammer them into thin sheets to cover the altar. They will be a sign to the Israelites."
39 So Eleazar the priest took the bronze censers. They had belonged to the men who were burned up. He had them hammered into sheets to cover the altar,

40 just as the LORD had told him through Moses. This was a reminder to the Israelites. No outsider, not from Aaron's family, should come near to offer incense to the LORD. If he does, he will become like Korah and his followers.
41 The next day all the people of Israel complained against Moses and Aaron. They said, "You have killed the LORD's people!"
42 But when the people gathered against them, Moses and Aaron turned toward the Tent of Meeting. Suddenly the cloud covered it. The glory of the LORD appeared.
43 Then Moses and Aaron went to the front of the Tent of Meeting.
44 The LORD said to Moses,
45 "Move away from these people. I will destroy them right away." Then Moses and Aaron fell to the ground with their faces down.

46 Moses said to Aaron, "Take your censer. Put fire from the altar in it. Add incense. Go quickly to the people and make things right for them. The LORD's anger has come out. The sickness has started."
47 So Aaron took the censer, as Moses had told him. He ran into the middle of the people. The sickness had already started among them. He offered the incense and made things right for the people.
48 He stood between the living and the dead. The sickness then stopped.
49 But 14,700 people had died from the sickness. This was besides those who died because of Korah.
50 Then Aaron returned to Moses at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting. The sickness had stopped.

title: Numbers 17
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Numbers
paragraphs: 1 The LORD said to Moses,
2 "Speak to the Israelites. Take twelve wooden staffs from them — one from the leader of each tribe. Write each man's name on his staff.
3 Write Aaron's name on the staff of Levi. There must be one staff for the head of each tribe.
4 Put the staffs in the Tent of Meeting, in front of the Testimony, where I meet with you.
5 The staff of the man I choose will start to grow. Then I will stop the constant complaining of the Israelites against you."
6 So Moses spoke to the Israelites. Each leader gave him a staff — one for each tribe, twelve staffs in all. Aaron's staff was among them.
7 Then Moses placed the staffs before the LORD in the Tent of the Testimony.
8 The next day Moses went into the Tent of the Testimony. He saw that Aaron's staff, for the family of Levi, had grown. It had pushed out buds, opened flowers, and grew almonds.
9 Then Moses brought out all the staffs from the LORD's presence to all the Israelites. They saw them, and each man took his own staff.
10 The LORD said to Moses, "Put Aaron's staff back in front of the Testimony. Keep it as a sign for the ones who refuse to obey. This will stop their complaining against me, so they will not die."
11 So Moses did just as the LORD had commanded him.
12 Then the Israelites said to Moses, "Look, we are dying! We are lost — we are all lost!
13 Anyone who comes near the tabernacle of the LORD will die. Are we all going to die?"

title: Numbers 18
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Numbers
paragraphs: 1 The LORD said to Aaron, "You and your sons and your father's family must carry the guilt for the sanctuary. You and your sons alone must carry the guilt for your priesthood.
2 Bring with you also your brothers from the tribe of Levi. They will join you and help you and your sons at the Tent of the Testimony.
3 They will care for your duties and all the duties of the Tent. But they must not come near the things of the sanctuary or the altar. If they do, both they and you will die.
4 They will join you and care for the duties of the Tent of Meeting. They will do all the work at the Tent. No outsider may come near you.
5 You must care for the duties of the sanctuary and the altar. Then my anger will not fall on the Israelites again.
6 Look, I have chosen your fellow Levites from the Israelites as a gift to you. They are set apart for the LORD to serve at the Tent of Meeting.

7 But only you and your sons may attend to your priesthood. This covers everything for the altar and inside the inner curtain. I give you the priesthood as a gift. Any outsider who comes near the sanctuary must be put to death."
8 Then the LORD said to Aaron, "Look, I have put you in charge of my offerings. I give you all the holy offerings of the Israelites as your share and a lasting law.
9 A share of the most holy offerings kept from the fire will be yours. From every grain offering, sin offering, and guilt offering, that part belongs to you and your sons.
10 You must eat it as a most holy offering. Every male may eat it. You must treat it as holy.
11 This is also yours: the lifted-up offerings of the Israelites. I give this to you and your sons and daughters as a lasting law. Every clean person in your house may eat it.
12 I give you all the best olive oil, the best new wine, and the best grain. The Israelites give these to the LORD as their firstfruits.
13 The firstfruits of everything in their land will belong to you. Every clean person in your house may eat them.
14 Everything in Israel given fully to the LORD belongs to you.
15 Every firstborn, whether human or animal, that is offered to the LORD belongs to you. But you must buy back every firstborn son. You must also buy back every firstborn male of unclean animals.
16 You must pay the price to buy back a month-old male. The set price is five silver coins. This follows the sanctuary coin, which is twenty small coins.

17 But you must not buy back the firstborn of an ox, a sheep, or a goat. They are holy. Sprinkle their blood on the altar and burn their fat as an offering by fire. It is a pleasing smell to the LORD.
18 Their meat belongs to you, just like the breast and right thigh of the lifted-up offering.

19 I give to you and your sons and daughters all the holy offerings the Israelites bring to the LORD. This is a lasting law. It is a lasting covenant of salt before the LORD for you and your children."
20 Then the LORD said to Aaron, "You will have no inheritance in their land. You will have no share among them. I am your share and your inheritance among the Israelites.
21 Look, I give the Levites all the tithes in Israel as their inheritance. This is their pay for the work they do at the Tent of Meeting.
22 The Israelites must no longer come near the Tent of Meeting. If they do, they will become guilty and die.
23 The Levites will do the work of the Tent of Meeting. They must carry the guilt for it. This is a lasting law for all generations. The Levites will receive no inheritance among the Israelites.
24 I give the Levites as their inheritance the tithe the Israelites bring to the LORD. That is why I told them they would receive no inheritance among the Israelites."
25 The LORD said to Moses,
26 "Speak to the Levites and tell them: 'You will receive the tithe from the Israelites as your inheritance. You must then give part of it as an offering to the LORD — a tenth of the tithe.
27 Your offering will be counted as grain from the place for grain or juice from the winepress.
28 So you must give an offering to the LORD from all the tithes you receive from the Israelites. From these, give the LORD's offering to Aaron the priest.
29 You must give the LORD's offering from the best of every gift, the holiest part of it.'
30 Therefore say to the Levites, 'When you have given the best part, the rest will be counted as the produce of the place for grain or the winepress.
31 You and your households may eat the rest anywhere. It is your pay for your work at the Tent of Meeting.
32 When you have given the best part, you will not become guilty. But you must not make the holy offerings of the Israelites unclean, or you will die.'"

title: Numbers 19
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Numbers
paragraphs: 1 Then the LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron.
2 "This is the rule of the law that the LORD commands. Tell the Israelites to bring you a red young cow. It must have nothing wrong with it. It must never have pulled a yoke.
3 Give the cow to Eleazar the priest. He will take it outside the camp and kill it there.
4 Eleazar the priest must take some of its blood on his finger. He must sprinkle it seven times toward the Tent of Meeting.
5 Then the cow must be burned in his sight. Burn its skin, its meat, its blood, and its waste.
6 The priest must take cedar wood, hyssop, and red wool. He must throw them onto the burning cow.
7 Then the priest must wash his clothes and bathe in water. After that he may come into the camp. But he will be unclean for worship until evening.
8 The man who burned the cow must also wash his clothes and bathe in water. He too will be unclean for worship until evening.

9 Then a clean man must gather the ashes of the cow. He must store them in a clean place outside the camp. The people of Israel will keep them. They will use them to make the water of purification. This water cleans away sin.
10 The man who gathered the ashes must also wash his clothes. He will be unclean for worship until evening. This rule lasts forever for the Israelites and for any foreigner who lives with them.
11 Anyone who touches a dead body will be unclean for seven days.
12 He must clean himself with the water on the third day and on the seventh day. Then he will be clean. But if he does not clean himself on those days, he will not be clean.

13 Anyone who touches a dead person and does not clean himself makes the tabernacle of the LORD unclean. That person must be sent away from Israel. He stays unclean. The water of purification was not sprinkled on him. His uncleanness is still on him.
14 This is the law when a person dies in a tent. Everyone who goes into the tent and everyone already inside will be unclean for seven days.
15 Any open jar without a lid on it is also unclean.

16 In the open field, anyone who touches someone killed by a sword will be unclean. The same rule covers anyone who touches a person who died, a human bone, or a grave. He will be unclean for seven days.
17 To clean an unclean person, take some ashes from the burned sin offering. Put them in a jar. Pour fresh water over them.

18 Then a clean man must take some hyssop. He must dip it in the water. He must sprinkle the tent, all the things inside, and the people who were there. He must also sprinkle the one who touched a bone, a grave, or a dead body.

19 The clean man must sprinkle the unclean person on the third day and on the seventh day. On the seventh day he will finish the cleaning. Then the unclean person must wash his clothes and bathe in water. That evening he will be clean.

20 But if an unclean person does not clean himself, he must be sent away from the people. He has made the sanctuary of the LORD unclean. The water of purification was not sprinkled on him. He is unclean.
21 This rule lasts forever for the people. The man who sprinkles the water of purification must wash his clothes. Anyone who touches this water will be unclean until evening.
22 Anything the unclean person touches will become unclean. Anyone who touches it will be unclean until evening."

title: Numbers 20
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Numbers
paragraphs: 1 In the first month, all the people of Israel came to the Wilderness of Zin. They stayed in Kadesh. There Miriam died and was buried.
2 There was no water for the people. So they gathered against Moses and Aaron.
3 The people argued with Moses. They said, "We wish we had died with our brothers before the LORD!
4 Why did you bring the LORD's people into this wilderness? Are we and our animals to die here?
5 Why did you bring us up out of Egypt to this terrible place? There is no grain, no figs, no vines, no pomegranates. And there is no water to drink!"
6 Then Moses and Aaron left the people. They went to the door of the Tent of Meeting. They fell facedown. The glory of the LORD appeared to them.
7 And the LORD said to Moses,

8 "Take the staff and gather the people. You and your brother Aaron must speak to that rock before their eyes. And it will pour out its water. You will bring water out of the rock for the people and their animals."
9 So Moses took the staff from before the LORD, just as he was told.
10 Then Moses and Aaron gathered the people in front of the rock. Moses said to them, "Listen, you rebels, must we bring you water out of this rock?"
