11 Then Moses raised his arm and struck the rock twice with his staff. Water gushed out, and the community and their livestock drank.
12 But the LORD said to Moses and Aaron, "Because you did not trust in me enough to honor me as holy in the sight of the Israelites, you will not bring this community into the land I give them."
13 These were the waters of Meribah. There the Israelites argued with the LORD. And he showed his holiness among them.
14 From Kadesh, Moses sent messengers to the king of Edom. He said, "This is what your brother Israel says: You know all the hard things we have suffered.
15 Our fathers went down to Egypt, and we lived there many years. The Egyptians treated us and our fathers badly.
16 When we cried out to the LORD, he heard our voice. He sent an angel and brought us out of Egypt. Now look, we are in Kadesh, a town at the edge of your land.

17 Please let us pass through your land. We will not go through any field or vineyard. We will not drink water from any well. We will stay on the King's Highway. We will not turn right or left until we pass through."
18 But Edom answered, "You may not pass through our land. If you try, we will attack you with swords."
19 The Israelites said, "We will stay on the main road. If we or our animals drink your water, we will pay for it. There will be no problem. Only let us pass through on foot."
20 But Edom said again, "You may not pass through." And they came out against Israel with a large and strong army.
21 So Edom refused to let Israel pass through their land. And Israel turned away from them.
22 They set out from Kadesh. All the people of Israel came to Mount Hor.
23 At Mount Hor, near the border of Edom, the LORD said to Moses and Aaron,
24 "Aaron will join his people who died. He will not enter the land I have given the Israelites. Both of you rebelled against my command at the waters of Meribah.
25 Take Aaron and his son Eleazar. Bring them up Mount Hor.
26 Take off Aaron's priestly clothes and put them on his son Eleazar. Aaron will join his people and die there."
27 So Moses did as the LORD commanded. They climbed Mount Hor in the sight of all the people.
28 Moses took off Aaron's clothes and put them on his son Eleazar. Then Aaron died there on top of the mountain. Moses and Eleazar came down from the mountain.
29 When all the people saw that Aaron had died, the whole house of Israel cried for him thirty days.

title: Numbers 21
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Numbers
paragraphs: 1 The Canaanite king of Arad lived in the Negev. He heard that Israel was coming on the road to Atharim. So he attacked Israel and took some prisoners.
2 Then Israel made a promise to the LORD. They said, "If you give this people into our hands, we will give their cities fully to you for destruction."
3 The LORD heard Israel's prayer and gave the Canaanites to them. Israel destroyed them and their cities. So they named the place Hormah.
4 Then they left Mount Hor and traveled on the road to the Red Sea. They went this way to go around the land of Edom. But the people became tired and angry on the journey.
5 They spoke against God and against Moses. They said, "Why did you bring us out of Egypt to die in the desert? There is no bread or water. We hate this terrible food!"
6 So the LORD sent snakes with deadly poison among the people. Many of the Israelites were bitten and died.

7 Then the people came to Moses. They said, "We have sinned. We spoke against the LORD and against you. Please pray to the LORD for us, so he will take the snakes away." So Moses prayed for the people.
8 Then the LORD said to Moses, "Make a snake and put it up on a pole. Anyone who is bitten can look at it and live."
9 So Moses made a bronze snake and put it up on a pole. Then when anyone was bitten by a snake and looked at the bronze snake, they lived.
10 Then the Israelites set out and camped at Oboth.
11 They traveled from Oboth and camped at Iye-abarim. This was in the desert east of Moab.
12 From there they moved on and camped in the Valley of Zered.
13 From there they moved on and camped on the other side of the Arnon. The Arnon is the border between Moab and the Amorites.
14 That is why the Book of the Wars of the LORD says: "Waheb in Suphah and the valleys of the Arnon,
15 even the slopes of the valleys that reach to Ar and lie along the border of Moab."
16 From there they went on to Beer. This is the well where the LORD said to Moses, "Gather the people, and I will give them water."
17 Then Israel sang this song: "Rise up, O well! All of you, sing to it!
18 The princes dug the well. The leaders of the people opened it with their royal sticks and staffs." Then the Israelites went from the desert to Mattanah,
19 and from Mattanah to Nahaliel, and from Nahaliel to Bamoth,
20 and from Bamoth to the valley in Moab. There the top of Pisgah looks down over the empty land.
21 Then Israel sent messengers to Sihon king of the Amorites. They said,
22 "Please let us pass through your land. We will not go into any field or vineyard. We will not drink water from any well. We will stay on the King's Highway until we pass through your land."
23 But Sihon would not let Israel pass through his land. Instead, he gathered his whole army. He went out to fight Israel in the desert. When he came to Jahaz, he fought against Israel.
24 Israel killed him with the sword and took his land. They took it from the Arnon to the Jabbok. But they only went up to the border of the Ammonites, because it was well-defended.
25 Israel captured all the cities of the Amorites and lived in them. This included Heshbon and all its villages.
26 Heshbon was the city of Sihon king of the Amorites. He had fought against the former king of Moab. He had taken all his land as far as the Arnon.
27 That is why the poets say: "Come to Heshbon, let it be rebuilt! Let the city of Sihon be restored!
28 For a fire went out from Heshbon. A blaze went out from the city of Sihon. It burned up Ar of Moab and the rulers of the heights of Arnon.
29 Sorrow to you, O Moab! You are destroyed, O people of Chemosh! He gave up his sons as people who run away. He gave up his daughters as prisoners to Sihon king of the Amorites.
30 But we have defeated them. Heshbon is destroyed as far as Dibon. We tore them down as far as Nophah, which reaches to Medeba."
31 So Israel lived in the land of the Amorites.
32 Moses sent spies to Jazer. Then Israel captured its villages and drove out the Amorites who were there.
33 Then they turned and went up the road to Bashan. Og king of Bashan and his whole army came out to fight them at Edrei.

34 But the LORD said to Moses, "Do not be afraid of him. I have given him into your hand, with all his people and his land. Do to him what you did to Sihon king of the Amorites, who lived in Heshbon."
35 So they struck down Og, with his sons and his whole army. No one was left alive. And they took his land.

title: Numbers 22
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Numbers
paragraphs: 1 Then the Israelites traveled on. They camped in the plains of Moab near the Jordan, across from Jericho.
2 Now Balak son of Zippor saw all that Israel had done to the Amorites.
3 Moab was very afraid of the people because they were so many. Moab was full of fear of the Israelites.
4 So the Moabites said to the elders of Midian, "This big crowd will eat up everything around us, like an ox eats the grass." Balak son of Zippor was king of Moab at that time.

5 He sent messengers to Balaam son of Beor at Pethor by the Euphrates River, in the land of his people. "Look, a people has come out of Egypt," said Balak. "They cover the land and have settled next to me.

6 So please come now and put a curse on this people. They are too strong for me. Maybe then I can defeat them and drive them out. I know that the people you bless are blessed, and the people you curse are cursed."
7 The elders of Moab and Midian left with money in hand to pay for magic words. They came to Balaam and told him the words of Balak.
8 "Stay here tonight," Balaam replied, "and I will tell you the answer the LORD speaks to me." So the leaders of Moab stayed with Balaam.
9 Then God came to Balaam and asked, "Who are these men with you?"
10 Balaam said to God, "Balak son of Zippor, king of Moab, sent me this message:
11 'Look, a people has come out of Egypt, and they cover the land. Now come and put a curse on them for me. Maybe I can fight them and drive them away.'"
12 But God said to Balaam, "Do not go with them. You must not put a curse on those people, because they are blessed."
13 So Balaam got up the next morning. He said to Balak's leaders, "Go back to your land, because the LORD has refused to let me go with you."
14 The leaders of Moab got up, returned to Balak, and said, "Balaam refused to come with us."
15 Then Balak sent other leaders, more in number and more important than the first ones.
16 They came to Balaam and said, "This is what Balak son of Zippor says: 'Please do not let anything stop you from coming to me.
17 I will give you great honor and do whatever you say. So please come and put a curse on this people for me!'"
18 But Balaam replied to the servants of Balak, "Even if Balak gave me all the silver and gold in his palace, I could not do anything great or small to go beyond the command of the LORD my God.
19 So please stay here tonight, like the others did. I will find out what else the LORD has to tell me."
20 That night God came to Balaam and said, "Since these men have come to call you, get up and go with them. But do only what I tell you."
21 So in the morning Balaam got up. He put a saddle on his donkey and went with the leaders of Moab.
22 Then God became angry because Balaam was going. The angel of the LORD stood in the road to stop him. Balaam was riding his donkey, and his two servants were with him.
23 The donkey saw the angel of the LORD standing in the road. He had a sword ready in his hand. She turned off the path and went into a field. So Balaam hit her to bring her back to the path.
24 Then the angel of the LORD stood in a narrow path between two vineyards, with walls on both sides.
25 The donkey saw the angel of the LORD and pressed herself against the wall. She pressed Balaam's foot hard against it. So he hit her again.
26 Then the angel of the LORD moved ahead and stood in a narrow place. There was no room to turn to the right or left.
27 When the donkey saw the angel of the LORD, she lay down under Balaam. He became very angry and hit her with his stick.
28 Then the LORD opened the donkey's mouth. She said to Balaam, "What have I done to you to make you beat me these three times?"
29 Balaam answered the donkey, "You have made me look foolish! If I had a sword in my hand, I would kill you right now!"
30 But the donkey said to Balaam, "Am I not the donkey you have ridden all your life until today? Have I ever done this to you before?" "No," he replied.
31 Then the LORD opened Balaam's eyes. He saw the angel of the LORD standing in the road with a sword ready in his hand. Balaam bowed down with his face to the ground.
32 The angel of the LORD asked him, "Why have you beaten your donkey these three times? Look, I have come out to stop you, because your way is reckless before me.
33 The donkey saw me and turned away from me these three times. If she had not turned away, I would certainly have killed you by now, but I would have spared her."
34 "I have sinned," Balaam said to the angel of the LORD. "I did not know you were standing in the road to stop me. Now, if this is not pleasing to you, I will go back home."
35 But the angel of the LORD said to Balaam, "Go with the men, but speak only what I tell you." So Balaam went with the leaders of Balak.
36 When Balak heard that Balaam was coming, he went out to meet him. He met him at the Moabite city on the Arnon border, at the edge of his land.
37 He said to Balaam, "Did I not send you a strong call to come? Why did you not come to me? Am I really not able to give you great gifts?"
38 "See, I have come to you," Balaam replied. "But can I say just anything? I must speak only the word that God puts in my mouth."
39 So Balaam went with Balak, and they came to Kiriath-huzoth.
40 Balak killed cattle and sheep as an offering. He gave parts of the meat to Balaam and the leaders with him.
41 The next morning, Balak took Balaam up to Bamoth-baal. From there he could see the edge of the camp of the people.

title: Numbers 23
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Numbers
paragraphs: 1 Then Balaam said to Balak, "Build me seven altars here. Prepare for me seven bulls and seven rams."
2 So Balak did what Balaam said. Balak and Balaam offered a bull and a ram on each altar.
3 Balaam said to Balak, "Stay here by your burnt offering while I am gone. Perhaps the LORD will meet with me. Whatever he shows me, I will tell you." So Balaam went off to a bare hilltop.
4 And God met with him. Balaam said, "I have set up seven altars. On each one I have offered a bull and a ram."
5 Then the LORD put a message in Balaam's mouth. He said, "Return to Balak and give him this message."
6 So Balaam returned to Balak. Balak was standing there beside his burnt offering, with all the leaders of Moab.
7 Then Balaam spoke this message from God: "Balak brought me from Aram, the king of Moab from the mountains of the east. 'Come,' he said, 'put a curse on Jacob for me. Come and speak against Israel!'
8 **How can I curse those whom God has not cursed?** How can I speak against those whom the LORD has not spoken against?
9 I see them from the top of the rocky cliffs. I watch them from the hills. Look, they are a people living alone. They do not count themselves among the nations.
10 Who can count the dust of Jacob? Who can count even a fourth of Israel? Let me die like the good people. Let my end be like theirs!"
11 Then Balak said to Balaam, "What have you done to me? I brought you here to curse my enemies. But look, you have only blessed them!"
12 But Balaam replied, "Should I not say exactly what the LORD puts in my mouth?"
13 Then Balak said to him, "Please come with me to another place. From there you will see them. You will only see the edge of their camp, not all of them. From there, curse them for me."
14 So Balak took him to the field of Zophim, to the top of Pisgah. There he built seven altars and offered a bull and a ram on each altar.
15 Balaam said to Balak, "Stay here beside your burnt offering while I meet the LORD over there."
16 And the LORD met with Balaam. He put a message in his mouth, saying, "Return to Balak and say what I tell you."
17 So Balaam returned to Balak. Balak was standing there by his burnt offering with the leaders of Moab. "What did the LORD say?" Balak asked.
18 Then Balaam spoke this message from God: "Rise up, O Balak, and listen. Hear me, O son of Zippor.
19 **God is not human, that he should lie, not a human being, that he should change his mind. Does he speak and then not act? Does he promise and not fulfill?**
20 I have received a command to bless. He has blessed, and I cannot change it.
21 He sees no disaster for Jacob. He sees no trouble for Israel. The LORD their God is with them. The shout of the King is among them.
22 God brought them out of Egypt with strength like a wild ox.
23 **There is no divination against Jacob, no evil omens against Israel. It will now be said of Jacob and of Israel, 'See what God has done!'**
24 Look, the people rise like a lioness. They lift themselves up like a lion. They will not rest until they eat what they catch and drink the blood of those they kill."
25 Then Balak said to Balaam, "Then do not curse them at all and do not bless them at all!"
26 But Balaam replied, "Did I not tell you? Whatever the LORD says, I must do."
27 Balak said, "Please come. I will take you to another place. Perhaps it will please God for you to curse them from there."
28 And Balak took Balaam to the top of Peor, which looks down over the empty land.
29 Then Balaam said, "Build me seven altars here. Prepare for me seven bulls and seven rams."
30 So Balak did what Balaam said. He offered a bull and a ram on each altar.

title: Numbers 24
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Numbers
paragraphs: 1 Balaam saw that it pleased the LORD to bless Israel. So this time he did not look for magic signs as before. He turned his face toward the wilderness.
2 When Balaam looked up, he saw Israel camped tribe by tribe. Then the Spirit of God came upon him,
3 and he spoke this message from God: "This is the message of Balaam son of Beor, the message of a man whose eyes are open,
4 the message of one who hears the words of God, who sees a vision from the Almighty, who falls down with eyes wide open:
5 **How beautiful are your tents, Jacob, your dwelling places, Israel!**
6 They spread out like rows of palm trees, like gardens beside a stream, like sweet-smelling plants the LORD has planted, like cedar trees beside the waters.
7 Water will flow from his buckets, and his seed will have plenty of water. His king will be greater than Agag, and his kingdom will be lifted high.
8 God brought him out of Egypt with strength like a wild ox. He will eat up enemy nations, crush their bones, and shoot them with arrows.
9 He lies down like a lion, like a lioness — who dares to wake him? Blessed are those who bless you, and cursed are those who curse you."
10 Then Balak's anger burned against Balaam. He clapped his hands in anger and said to Balaam, "I called you to curse my enemies. But look — you have blessed them three times!
11 Now flee at once to your home! I said I would pay you well. But the LORD has kept your reward from you."
12 Balaam answered Balak, "Did I not tell the messengers you sent me?
13 I said, 'Even if Balak gives me his house full of silver and gold, I cannot go beyond what the LORD says. I cannot do anything good or bad on my own. I will speak only what the LORD says.'
14 Now I am going back to my people. But come — let me warn you what this people will do to your people in the days to come."
15 Then Balaam spoke this message from God: "This is the message of Balaam son of Beor, the message of a man whose eyes are open,
16 the message of one who hears the words of God, who has knowledge from the Most High, who sees a vision from the Almighty, who falls down with eyes wide open:
17 I see him, but not now. I see him, but he is not near. **A star will come out of Jacob; a scepter will rise out of Israel.** He will crush the heads of Moab and strike down all the sons of Sheth.
18 Edom will be taken over. Seir, his enemy, will be taken too. But Israel will act with great strength.
19 A ruler will come from Jacob. He will destroy those left in the city."
20 Then Balaam saw Amalek and spoke this message from God: "Amalek was first among the nations, but his end will be ruin."
21 Next he saw the Kenites and spoke this message from God: "Your home is safe, and your nest is built in a rock.
22 Yet Kain will be destroyed when Asshur takes you away."
23 Once more Balaam spoke this message from God: "Ah, who can live unless God has planned it?
24 Ships will come from the shores of Cyprus. They will bring down Asshur and Eber, but they too will be destroyed forever."
25 Then Balaam got up and returned to his home. And Balak also went on his way.

title: Numbers 25
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Numbers
paragraphs: 1 While Israel stayed at Shittim, the men began to do sexual sin with the women of Moab.
2 These women invited them to offerings for their gods. The people ate and bowed down to their gods.
3 So Israel joined in worship of Baal of Peor. The LORD became very angry with them.
4 Then the LORD said to Moses, "Take all the leaders of the people. Kill them in the open day before the LORD. Then his fierce anger will turn away from Israel."
5 So Moses told the judges of Israel, "Each of you must kill your men who joined in worship of Baal of Peor."
6 Just then an Israelite man brought a Midianite woman to his family. He did this in front of Moses and the whole crowd of Israel. They were weeping at the door of the Tent of Meeting.
7 Phinehas son of Eleazar, the son of Aaron the priest, saw this. He got up from the crowd and took a spear in his hand.
8 He followed the Israelite into the tent. He pushed the spear through them both — through the Israelite and into the woman's belly. So the plague against the Israelites stopped.
9 But those who died in the plague were 24,000.
10 Then the LORD said to Moses,
11 "Phinehas son of Eleazar, the son of Aaron the priest, has turned my anger away from the Israelites. He was full of zeal for me among them. So I did not destroy the Israelites in my zeal.
12 So tell him this: I am giving him my covenant of peace.
13 It will be a covenant of priesthood that lasts forever for him and his children. He was full of zeal for his God and made atonement for the Israelites."
14 The name of the Israelite killed with the Midianite woman was Zimri son of Salu. He was a leader of a family from Simeon.
15 The name of the Midianite woman who was killed was Cozbi. She was the daughter of Zur, a tribal leader of a Midianite family.
16 And the LORD said to Moses,
17 "Attack the Midianites and strike them dead.

18 They attacked you with tricks. They led you into sin in the matter of Peor. And they used their sister Cozbi, the daughter of the Midianite leader. She was the woman killed on the day the plague came because of Peor."

title: Numbers 26
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Numbers
paragraphs: 1 After the sickness ended, the LORD spoke to Moses and Eleazar son of Aaron the priest.
2 "Count all the people of Israel by their family groups. Count every man twenty years old or older who can fight in Israel's army."
3 So on the plains of Moab by the Jordan, across from Jericho, Moses and Eleazar the priest gave the order.
4 "Count the men twenty years old or older, as the LORD commanded Moses." These were the people of Israel who came out of Egypt:
5 Reuben was the firstborn son of Israel. These were Reuben's sons: the family group of Hanoch, the family group of Pallu,
6 the family group of Hezron, and the family group of Carmi.
7 These were the family groups of Reuben. Their count was 43,730.
8 The son of Pallu was Eliab.

9 The sons of Eliab were Nemuel, Dathan, and Abiram. Dathan and Abiram were chosen by the people. They fought against Moses and Aaron with the people who followed Korah. They rebelled against the LORD.
10 The ground opened up and took them in with Korah. Fire burned up 250 men who followed him. They serve as a warning to others.
11 But Korah's family line did not end.
12 These were the sons of Simeon by their family groups: the family group of Nemuel, the family group of Jamin, the family group of Jachin,
13 the family group of Zerah, and the family group of Shaul.
14 These were the family groups of Simeon. There were 22,200 men.
15 These were the sons of Gad by their family groups: the family group of Zephon, the family group of Haggi, the family group of Shuni,
16 the family group of Ozni, the family group of Eri,
17 the family group of Arod, and the family group of Areli.
18 These were the family groups of Gad. Their count was 40,500.
19 The sons of Judah were Er and Onan. But they died in the land of Canaan.
20 These were the sons of Judah by their family groups: the family group of Shelah, the family group of Perez, and the family group of Zerah.
21 These were the sons of Perez: the family group of Hezron and the family group of Hamul.
22 These were the family groups of Judah. Their count was 76,500.
23 These were the sons of Issachar by their family groups: the family group of Tola, the family group of Puvah,
24 the family group of Jashub, and the family group of Shimron.
25 These were the family groups of Issachar. Their count was 64,300.
26 These were the sons of Zebulun by their family groups: the family group of Sered, the family group of Elon, and the family group of Jahleel.
27 These were the family groups of Zebulun. Their count was 60,500.
28 The sons of Joseph included the family groups of Manasseh and Ephraim.
29 These were the sons of Manasseh: the family group of Machir, the father of Gilead, and the family group of Gilead.
30 These were the sons of Gilead: the family group of Iezer, the family group of Helek,
31 the family group of Asriel, the family group of Shechem,
32 the family group of Shemida, and the family group of Hepher.
33 Zelophehad son of Hepher had no sons but only daughters. Their names were Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah.
34 These were the family groups of Manasseh. Their count was 52,700.
35 These were the sons of Ephraim by their family groups: the family group of Shuthelah, the family group of Becher, and the family group of Tahan.
36 The sons of Shuthelah were the family group of Eran.
37 These were the family groups of Ephraim. Their count was 32,500. These family groups came from Joseph.
38 These were the sons of Benjamin by their family groups: the family group of Bela, the family group of Ashbel, the family group of Ahiram,
39 the family group of Shupham, and the family group of Hupham.
40 The sons of Bela from Ard and Naaman were the family group of Ard and the family group of Naaman.
41 These were the family groups of Benjamin. Their count was 45,600.
42 These were the sons of Dan by their family groups: the family group of Shuham. These were the family groups of Dan.
43 All of them were family groups of Shuham. Their count was 64,400.
44 These were the sons of Asher by their family groups: the family group of Imnah, the family group of Ishvi, and the family group of Beriah.
45 These were the sons of Beriah: the family group of Heber and the family group of Malchiel.
46 The name of Asher's daughter was Serah.
47 These were the family groups of Asher. Their count was 53,400.
48 These were the sons of Naphtali by their family groups: the family group of Jahzeel, the family group of Guni,
49 the family group of Jezer, and the family group of Shillem.
50 These were the family groups of Naphtali. Their count was 45,400.
51 The total count of the men of Israel was 601,730.
52 Then the LORD said to Moses,
53 "Divide the land among the tribes as their share. Give it according to the number of names.
54 Give a larger share to a big tribe. Give a smaller share to a small tribe. Each tribe gets land based on its recorded number.
55 You must divide the land by drawing lots. They will get their share by the names of their fathers' tribes.
56 Divide each share by drawing lots among the bigger and smaller tribes."
57 These were the Levites counted by their family groups: the family group of Gershon, the family group of Kohath, and the family group of Merari.
58 These were the families of the Levites: the family of Libni, the family of Hebron, the family of Mahli, the family of Mushi, and the family of Korah. Kohath was the father of Amram.
59 Amram's wife was named Jochebed. She was a daughter of Levi, born to Levi in Egypt. She gave Amram three children: Aaron, Moses, and their sister Miriam.
60 Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar were born to Aaron.
61 But Nadab and Abihu died. They offered fire that God did not allow before the LORD.
62 The count of the Levites was 23,000, every male a month old or older. They were not counted with the other Israelites. They did not get a share of land among them.
63 Moses and Eleazar the priest counted these people on the plains of Moab. They counted them by the Jordan, across from Jericho.
64 Not one of these men had been counted by Moses and Aaron the priest before. That earlier count happened in the Sinai Desert.
65 For the LORD had told them they would surely die in the desert. Only Caleb son of Jephunneh and Joshua son of Nun were left.

title: Numbers 27
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Numbers
paragraphs: 1 Zelophehad had five daughters. He was the son of Hepher, the son of Gilead, the son of Machir, the son of Manasseh. They belonged to the family groups of Manasseh, son of Joseph. Their names were Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah. They came
2 to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. They stood before Moses, Eleazar the priest, the leaders, and all the people. They said,
3 "Our father died in the wilderness. But he was not in the group of Korah, who joined against the LORD. He died because of his own sin. And he had no sons.
4 Why should our father's name disappear from his family group? He had no sons. Please give us land among our father's brothers."
5 So Moses brought their case before the LORD.
6 And the LORD answered him,
7 "The daughters of Zelophehad are right. You must give them land among their father's brothers. Pass their father's land to them.
8 Also, say this to the Israelites: 'If a man dies and leaves no son, pass his land to his daughter.
9 If he has no daughter, give his land to his brothers.
10 If he has no brothers, give his land to his father's brothers.

11 If his father has no brothers, give his land to the closest male relative in his family group. That man will take it as his own. This is a fixed law for the Israelites, as the LORD has commanded Moses.'"
12 Then the LORD said to Moses, "Go up this mountain in the Abarim mountains. Look at the land that I have given the Israelites.
13 After you see it, you also will join your people who have died, as your brother Aaron did.

14 In the Wilderness of Zin, all the people argued with me. Both of you did not obey my command. You did not honor me as holy before them at the waters." Those were the waters of Meribah in Kadesh, in the Wilderness of Zin.
15 So Moses prayed to the LORD,
16 "May the LORD, the God who gives life to all people, choose a man to lead the people.
17 He will go out and come in before them. He will lead them out and bring them in. Then the people of the LORD will not be like sheep with no shepherd."
18 The LORD said to Moses, "Take Joshua son of Nun. He is a man who has the Spirit in him. Put your hands on him.
19 Have him stand before Eleazar the priest and all the people. Give him his task in front of them.
20 Give him some of your power. Then all the people of Israel will obey him.
21 He will stand before Eleazar the priest. The priest will ask the LORD for guidance by using the Urim. At his command, Joshua and all the Israelites will go out and come in."
22 Moses did as the LORD had commanded him. He took Joshua. He had him stand before Eleazar the priest and all the people.
23 He put his hands on Joshua and gave him his task, as the LORD had said through Moses.

title: Numbers 28
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Numbers
paragraphs: 1 Then the LORD said to Moses,
2 "Give this command to the people of Israel. Tell them, 'Bring to Me at the right time the food for My fire offerings. It will be a pleasing smell to Me.'
3 Tell them this is the fire offering you must bring to the LORD. Each day bring two male lambs, one year old, with no faults. This is the daily burnt offering.
4 Offer one lamb in the morning. Offer the other lamb in the evening.
5 Also bring a grain offering. Use a tenth of an ephah of fine flour, mixed with a quarter hin of olive oil.
6 This is the daily burnt offering set up at Mount Sinai. It is a pleasing smell, a fire offering to the LORD.
7 With each lamb, bring a drink offering of a quarter hin. Pour out the strong drink to the LORD in the holy place.
8 Offer the second lamb in the evening. Bring the same grain offering and drink offering as in the morning. It is a fire offering, a pleasing smell to the LORD.
9 On the Sabbath day, bring two male lambs one year old, with no faults. Also bring a grain offering of two-tenths of an ephah of fine flour mixed with oil. Bring a drink offering too.
10 This is the burnt offering for every Sabbath. Bring it along with the daily burnt offering and its drink offering.
11 On the first day of each month, bring a burnt offering to the LORD. Bring two young bulls, one ram, and seven male lambs one year old. All must have no faults.
12 With each bull, bring three-tenths of an ephah of fine flour mixed with oil as a grain offering. With the ram, bring two-tenths of an ephah of fine flour mixed with oil.
13 With each lamb, bring a tenth of an ephah of fine flour mixed with oil as a grain offering. This is a burnt offering, a pleasing smell, a fire offering to the LORD.
14 The drink offerings will be half a hin of wine with each bull, a third of a hin with the ram, and a quarter hin with each lamb. This is the monthly burnt offering for each new moon all year.
15 Also bring one male goat to the LORD as a sin offering. Bring it along with the daily burnt offering and its drink offering.
16 The fourteenth day of the first month is the LORD's Passover.
17 On the fifteenth day of this month, there will be a feast. For seven days you must eat bread with no yeast.
18 On the first day, hold a holy meeting. You must not do any normal work.
19 Bring a fire offering to the LORD. Offer two young bulls, one ram, and seven male lambs one year old. All must have no faults.
20 For the grain offering, use fine flour mixed with oil. Bring three-tenths of an ephah with each bull, two-tenths of an ephah with the ram,
21 and a tenth of an ephah with each of the seven lambs.
22 Also bring one male goat as a sin offering to cover your sins.
23 Bring all these along with the regular morning burnt offering.
24 Offer the same food each day for seven days. It is a fire offering, a pleasing smell to the LORD. Bring it with its drink offering and the daily burnt offering.
25 On the seventh day, hold a holy meeting. You must not do any normal work.
26 On the day of firstfruits, bring an offering of new grain to the LORD. This is during the Feast of Weeks. Hold a holy meeting. You must not do any normal work.
27 Bring a burnt offering as a pleasing smell to the LORD. Offer two young bulls, one ram, and seven male lambs one year old.
28 Bring grain offerings of fine flour mixed with oil — three-tenths of an ephah with each bull, two-tenths of an ephah with the ram,
29 and a tenth of an ephah with each of the seven lambs.
30 Also bring one male goat to cover your sins.
31 Offer these with their drink offerings, along with the daily burnt offering and its grain offering. The animals must have no faults.

title: Numbers 29
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Numbers
paragraphs: 1 "On the first day of the seventh month, you must hold a sacred meeting. Do not do your usual work. On that day you will blow the trumpets.
2 Bring a burnt offering as a sweet smell that pleases the LORD. Offer one young bull, one ram, and seven male lambs one year old. All must have no defects.
3 With them bring grain offerings of fine flour mixed with oil. Use three-tenths of an ephah (about 6 liters) with the bull. Use two-tenths of an ephah (about 4 liters) with the ram.
4 Use a tenth of an ephah (about 2 liters) with each of the seven male lambs.
5 Also bring one male goat as a sin offering to cover your sins.
6 These are as well as the monthly and daily burnt offerings. Bring them with their grain offerings and drink offerings. They are a sweet smell, an offering made by fire to the LORD.
7 "On the tenth day of this seventh month, you must hold a sacred meeting. Humble yourselves before God. Do not do any work.
8 Bring a burnt offering as a sweet smell that pleases the LORD. Offer one young bull, one ram, and seven male lambs one year old. All must have no defects.
9 With them bring grain offerings of fine flour mixed with oil. Use three-tenths of an ephah with the bull. Use two-tenths of an ephah with the ram.
10 Use a tenth of an ephah with each of the seven lambs.
11 Also bring one male goat as a sin offering. Bring it as well as the sin offering to cover your sins. Bring it as well as the regular burnt offering with its grain offering and drink offerings.
12 "On the fifteenth day of the seventh month, you must hold a sacred meeting. Do not do your usual work. You will hold a feast to the LORD for seven days.
13 Bring an offering made by fire as a sweet smell that pleases the LORD. Offer thirteen young bulls, two rams, and fourteen male lambs one year old. All must have no defects.
14 With them bring a grain offering of fine flour mixed with oil. Use three-tenths of an ephah with each of the thirteen bulls. Use two-tenths of an ephah with each of the two rams.
15 Use a tenth of an ephah with each of the fourteen lambs.
16 Also bring one male goat as a sin offering. Bring it as well as the regular burnt offering with its grain offering and drink offering.
17 "On the second day bring twelve young bulls, two rams, and fourteen male lambs one year old. All must have no defects.
18 Bring the grain and drink offerings for the bulls, the rams, and the lambs, as the rules tell you.
19 Also bring one male goat as a sin offering. Bring it as well as the regular burnt offering with its grain offering and drink offering.
20 "On the third day bring eleven bulls, two rams, and fourteen male lambs one year old. All must have no defects.
21 Bring the grain and drink offerings for the bulls, the rams, and the lambs, as the rules tell you.
22 Also bring one male goat as a sin offering. Bring it as well as the regular burnt offering with its grain offering and drink offering.
23 "On the fourth day bring ten bulls, two rams, and fourteen male lambs one year old. All must have no defects.
24 Bring the grain and drink offerings for the bulls, the rams, and the lambs, as the rules tell you.
25 Also bring one male goat as a sin offering. Bring it as well as the regular burnt offering with its grain offering and drink offering.
26 "On the fifth day bring nine bulls, two rams, and fourteen male lambs one year old. All must have no defects.
27 Bring the grain and drink offerings for the bulls, the rams, and the lambs, as the rules tell you.
28 Also bring one male goat as a sin offering. Bring it as well as the regular burnt offering with its grain offering and drink offering.
29 "On the sixth day bring eight bulls, two rams, and fourteen male lambs one year old. All must have no defects.
30 Bring the grain and drink offerings for the bulls, the rams, and the lambs, as the rules tell you.
31 Also bring one male goat as a sin offering. Bring it as well as the regular burnt offering with its grain offering and drink offering.
32 "On the seventh day bring seven bulls, two rams, and fourteen male lambs one year old. All must have no defects.
33 Bring the grain and drink offerings for the bulls, the rams, and the lambs, as the rules tell you.
34 Also bring one male goat as a sin offering. Bring it as well as the regular burnt offering with its grain offering and drink offering.
35 "On the eighth day you must hold a special meeting. Do not do your usual work.
36 Bring an offering made by fire as a sweet smell that pleases the LORD. Offer one bull, one ram, and seven male lambs one year old. All must have no defects.
37 Bring the grain and drink offerings for the bull, the ram, and the lambs, as the rules tell you.
38 Also bring one male goat as a sin offering. Bring it as well as the regular burnt offering with its grain offering and drink offering.

39 "Bring these offerings to the LORD at your set times. Bring them as well as the offerings you promise and the offerings you give freely. These can be burnt offerings, grain offerings, drink offerings, or peace offerings."
40 So Moses told all this to the Israelites, just as the LORD had commanded him.

title: Numbers 30
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Numbers
paragraphs: 1 Then Moses spoke to the leaders of the tribes of Israel. He said, "This is what the LORD has commanded:
2 If a man makes a vow to the LORD, he must keep it. If he makes a strong promise to tie himself by a pledge, he must keep it. He must not break his word. He must do everything he promised.
3 A young woman may still live in her father's house. She may make a vow to the LORD or tie herself by a pledge.
4 Her father may hear about the vow or pledge. If he says nothing to her, then all her vows and pledges will stand.
5 But her father may tell her no on the day he hears. Then none of her vows or pledges will stand. The LORD will set her free, because her father told her no.
6 A woman may marry while she is under a vow. She may also be under a quick promise that ties her.
7 Her husband may hear of it but say nothing to her that day. Then her vows and pledges will stand.
8 But her husband may tell her no when he hears of it. Then he cancels the vow that ties her. He cancels the quick promise she made. And the LORD will set her free.
9 Every vow a widow or divorced woman makes will stand. She must keep it.
10 A woman may live in her husband's house. She may make a vow or tie herself by a strong promise.
11 Her husband may hear of it but say nothing. He may not tell her no. Then all her vows and pledges will stand.

12 But her husband may cancel them on the day he hears. Then nothing she said with her lips will stand. Her vows and pledges will not stand. Her husband has cancelled them. And the LORD will set her free.
13 Her husband may accept or cancel any vow she makes. He may accept or cancel any promise to give something up.
14 But her husband may say nothing to her from day to day. Then he accepts all her vows and pledges. He has accepted them by saying nothing on the day he heard.
15 But he may cancel them later, after he has heard of them. Then he will carry her guilt."
16 These are the rules the LORD gave Moses. They are about a man and his wife. They are about a father and his young daughter at home.

title: Numbers 31
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Numbers
paragraphs: 1 The LORD said to Moses,
2 "Pay back the Midianites for what they did to Israel. After that, you will join your people who have died."
3 So Moses told the people, "Get some men ready for war. Send them against the Midianites to carry out the LORD's punishment.
4 Send a thousand men from each tribe of Israel into battle."
5 So they chose a thousand men from each tribe of Israel — twelve thousand men ready for war.
6 Moses sent the thousand men from each tribe into battle. Phinehas son of Eleazar the priest went with them. He took the holy objects and the trumpets to give signals.
7 They fought against Midian, just as the LORD had told Moses. They killed every male.
8 They killed the five kings of Midian: Evi, Rekem, Zur, Hur, and Reba. They also killed Balaam son of Beor with the sword.
9 The Israelites captured the Midianite women and their children. They took all their cattle, sheep, and other things.
10 Then they burned all the towns where the Midianites had lived. They also burned all their camps.
11 They carried away all the things and people they had taken — both people and animals.

12 They brought the captured people, animals, and goods to Moses, to Eleazar the priest, and to the community of Israel. They brought them to the camp on the plains of Moab, by the Jordan across from Jericho.
13 Moses, Eleazar the priest, and all the leaders of the community went to meet them outside the camp.
14 But Moses was angry with the leaders of the army — the commanders of thousands and the commanders of hundreds — who came back from the battle.
15 "Have you left all the women alive?" he asked them.
16 "Look, these women followed Balaam's advice. They made the people of Israel turn away from the LORD at Peor. So sickness came on the LORD's community.
17 So now, kill all the boys. Also kill every woman who has slept with a man.
18 But keep alive for yourselves every girl who has never slept with a man.
19 All of you who have killed a person or touched a dead body must stay outside the camp for seven days. On the third day and the seventh day, make yourselves and your captives clean.
20 Also make clean every piece of clothing and every leather thing. Clean everything made of goat's hair and every wooden thing."
21 Then Eleazar the priest said to the soldiers who had gone into battle, "This is the rule of the law that the LORD gave to Moses.
22 The gold, silver, bronze, iron, tin, and lead —

23 everything that can survive the fire — must go through the fire. Then it will be clean. But you must still wash it with the water that makes things clean. Everything that cannot survive the fire must go through the water.
24 On the seventh day, wash your clothes, and you will be clean. After that, you may come into the camp."
25 The LORD said to Moses,
26 "You and Eleazar the priest and the family leaders of the community must count what you captured. Count both people and animals.
27 Then divide them between the soldiers who went to war and the rest of the community.
28 Keep apart a gift for the LORD from the soldiers' share. Take one out of every five hundred — people, cattle, donkeys, or sheep.
29 Take it from their half. Give it to Eleazar the priest as an offering to the LORD.
30 From the Israelites' half, take one out of every fifty. Take from people, cattle, donkeys, sheep, or other animals. Give them to the Levites who take care of the LORD's tent."
31 So Moses and Eleazar the priest did what the LORD had told Moses.
32 This is what was left from the things the soldiers had taken: 675,000 sheep,
33 72,000 cattle,
34 61,000 donkeys,
35 and 32,000 women who had never slept with a man.
36 This was the half for those who went to war: 337,500 sheep.
37 The gift to the LORD from the sheep was 675.
38 There were 36,000 cattle. The gift to the LORD was 72.
39 There were 30,500 donkeys. The gift to the LORD was 61.
40 There were 16,000 people. The gift to the LORD was 32.
41 Moses gave the gift to Eleazar the priest as an offering for the LORD. He did this just as the LORD had told him.
42 Moses kept the Israelites' half apart from the share for the men who went to war.
43 This half belonged to the community: 337,500 sheep,
44 36,000 cattle,
45 30,500 donkeys,
46 and 16,000 people.
47 From the Israelites' half, Moses took one out of every fifty people and animals. He gave them to the Levites who took care of the LORD's tent. He did this just as the LORD had told him.
48 Then the leaders over the army units came to Moses. They were the commanders of thousands and of hundreds.
49 They said, "Your servants have counted the soldiers under our command. Not one of us is missing.
50 So we have brought an offering to the LORD. Each man gave the gold things he got — armlets, bracelets, rings, earrings, and necklaces. We bring them to pay for our sins before the LORD."
51 So Moses and Eleazar the priest took all the gold things from them.
52 All the gold that the commanders gave as an offering to the LORD weighed 16,750 shekels.
53 Each soldier had taken things for himself.
54 Moses and Eleazar the priest took the gold from the commanders of thousands and of hundreds. They brought it into the Tent of Meeting as a reminder for the Israelites before the LORD.

title: Numbers 32
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Numbers
paragraphs: 1 The people of Reuben and the people of Gad had many cows and sheep. They looked at the lands of Jazer and Gilead. They saw that the land was good for animals.
2 So the people of Gad and Reuben came to Moses, Eleazar the priest, and the leaders of the people.
3 They said, "Ataroth, Dibon, Jazer, Nimrah, Heshbon, Elealeh, Sebam, Nebo, and Beon —
4 the land that the LORD took for the people of Israel — is good for animals. And we, your servants, have many animals."
5 They said, "If you are pleased with us, please give this land to us as our own. Do not make us cross the Jordan."
6 But Moses asked the people of Gad and Reuben, "Should your brothers go to war while you sit here?
7 Why are you making the people of Israel afraid to cross into the land that the LORD has given them?
8 This is what your fathers did. I sent them from Kadesh-barnea to look at the land.
9 They went up to the Valley of Eshcol and saw the land. Then they made the people of Israel afraid to enter the land that the LORD had given them.
10 So the LORD became very angry that day. He made a strong promise.
11 He said, 'They did not follow me with all their heart. So not one man twenty years old or older who came out of Egypt will see the land. I promised this land to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.
12 Only Caleb son of Jephunneh the Kenizzite and Joshua son of Nun will see it. They followed the LORD with all their heart.'
13 The LORD's anger burned against Israel. He made them move around in the desert for forty years. He waited until all the people who did evil were gone.
14 Now look — you are a group of sinners. You stand in your fathers' place. You make the LORD even more angry with Israel.
15 If you turn away from following him, he will leave this people in the desert again. And you will cause their ruin."
16 Then the people of Gad and Reuben came to Moses. They said, "We want to build safe places here for our animals and cities for our children.

17 But we will take our weapons and go before the people of Israel. We will lead them until we bring them into their place. Meanwhile, our children will stay in strong walled cities. They will be safe from the people of the land.
18 We will not return to our homes until every Israelite has his own land.
19 We will not take land with them across the Jordan. Our land is here on the east side of the Jordan."
20 Moses replied, "If you will do this — if you will take your weapons for battle before the LORD,
21 and if every armed man crosses the Jordan before the LORD, until he drives out his enemies,
22 then when the land is under the LORD's power, you may return. You will be free from your duty to the LORD and to Israel. And this land will be yours before the LORD.
23 But if you do not do this, you will sin against the LORD. Be sure that your sin will catch you.
24 Build cities for your children and folds for your sheep. But do what you have promised."
25 The people of Gad and Reuben said to Moses, "Your servants will do just as our lord commands.
26 Our children, our wives, our cows, and all our animals will stay here in the cities of Gilead.
27 But your servants are ready for war. Every man will cross over to fight before the LORD, just as our lord says."
28 So Moses gave orders about them to Eleazar the priest, to Joshua son of Nun, and to the family leaders of the tribes of Israel.

29 Moses said to them, "The people of Gad and Reuben must cross the Jordan with you. Every man must be armed for battle before the LORD. When the land is under your power, give them the land of Gilead as their own.
30 But if they do not arm themselves and cross over with you, they must take their land among you in Canaan."
31 The people of Gad and Reuben replied, "We will do what the LORD has told your servants.
32 We will cross over into Canaan armed before the LORD. Then our land will be on this side of the Jordan."

33 So Moses gave land to the people of Gad, the people of Reuben, and half of the tribe of Manasseh son of Joseph. He gave them the kingdom of Sihon king of the Amorites and the kingdom of Og king of Bashan. This included the cities and the land around them.
34 The people of Gad built up Dibon, Ataroth, Aroer,
35 Atroth-shophan, Jazer, Jogbehah,
36 Beth-nimrah, and Beth-haran as strong walled cities. They also built folds for their sheep.
37 The people of Reuben built up Heshbon, Elealeh, Kiriathaim,
38 Nebo, Baal-meon, and Sibmah. They gave new names to Nebo and Baal-meon. They renamed the cities they rebuilt.
39 The descendants of Machir son of Manasseh went to Gilead. They took it and drove out the Amorites who lived there.
40 So Moses gave Gilead to the family of Machir son of Manasseh. They settled there.
41 Jair, a descendant of Manasseh, went and took their villages. He called them Havvoth-jair.
42 And Nobah went and took Kenath and its villages. He named it Nobah, after his own name.

title: Numbers 33
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Numbers
paragraphs: 1 These are the journeys of the Israelites. They came out of Egypt group by group, led by Moses and Aaron.
2 The LORD told Moses to write down each stop on their journey. Here are the stops, starting from the beginning:
3 On the fifteenth day of the first month, the day after the Passover, the Israelites left Rameses. They marched out boldly while all the Egyptians watched.
4 The Egyptians were burying all their firstborn sons. The LORD had struck them down. The LORD had also judged their gods.
5 The Israelites left Rameses and camped at Succoth.
6 They left Succoth and camped at Etham, at the edge of the wilderness.
7 They left Etham and turned back to Pi-hahiroth, near Baal-zephon. They camped near Migdol.
8 They left Pi-hahiroth and crossed through the sea into the wilderness. They walked three days into the Wilderness of Etham and camped at Marah.
9 They left Marah and came to Elim. Elim had twelve springs and seventy palm trees. They camped there.
10 They left Elim and camped by the Red Sea.
11 They left the Red Sea and camped in the Desert of Sin.
12 They left the Desert of Sin and camped at Dophkah.
13 They left Dophkah and camped at Alush.
14 They left Alush and camped at Rephidim. There was no water for the people to drink there.
15 They left Rephidim and camped in the Wilderness of Sinai.
16 They left the Wilderness of Sinai and camped at Kibroth-hattaavah.
17 They left Kibroth-hattaavah and camped at Hazeroth.
18 They left Hazeroth and camped at Rithmah.
19 They left Rithmah and camped at Rimmon-perez.
20 They left Rimmon-perez and camped at Libnah.
21 They left Libnah and camped at Rissah.
22 They left Rissah and camped at Kehelathah.
23 They left Kehelathah and camped at Mount Shepher.
24 They left Mount Shepher and camped at Haradah.
25 They left Haradah and camped at Makheloth.
26 They left Makheloth and camped at Tahath.
27 They left Tahath and camped at Terah.
28 They left Terah and camped at Mithkah.
29 They left Mithkah and camped at Hashmonah.
30 They left Hashmonah and camped at Moseroth.
31 They left Moseroth and camped at Bene-jaakan.
32 They left Bene-jaakan and camped at Hor-haggidgad.
33 They left Hor-haggidgad and camped at Jotbathah.
34 They left Jotbathah and camped at Abronah.
35 They left Abronah and camped at Ezion-geber.
36 They left Ezion-geber and camped at Kadesh in the Wilderness of Zin.
37 They left Kadesh and camped at Mount Hor, at the edge of the land of Edom.
38 At the LORD's command, Aaron the priest climbed Mount Hor. He died there on the first day of the fifth month. It was the fortieth year after the Israelites came out of Egypt.
39 Aaron was 123 years old when he died on Mount Hor.
40 The Canaanite king of Arad lived in the Negev in the land of Canaan. He heard that the Israelites were coming.
41 The Israelites left Mount Hor and camped at Zalmonah.
42 They left Zalmonah and camped at Punon.
43 They left Punon and camped at Oboth.
44 They left Oboth and camped at Iye-abarim on the border of Moab.
45 They left Iyim and camped at Dibon-gad.
46 They left Dibon-gad and camped at Almon-diblathaim.
47 They left Almon-diblathaim and camped in the mountains of Abarim, facing Nebo.
48 They left the mountains of Abarim and camped on the plains of Moab. The plains were by the Jordan, across from Jericho.
49 They camped there by the Jordan, from Beth-jeshimoth to Abel-shittim.
50 On the plains of Moab by the Jordan, across from Jericho, the LORD said to Moses,
51 "Speak to the Israelites. Tell them: When you cross the Jordan into the land of Canaan,
52 you must drive out all the people who live there. Destroy all their carved idols and metal idols. Tear down all their places of worship.
53 You will take the land and live in it. I have given you the land to own.

54 Divide the land by drawing lots, by your clans. Give more land to a larger clan and less land to a smaller clan. Each clan will get what the lot decides. Divide it among the tribes of your families.
55 But you must drive out the people who live there. If you let them stay, they will become sharp pains in your eyes and your sides. They will trouble you in the land where you live.
56 Then I will do to you what I planned to do to them."

title: Numbers 34
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Numbers
paragraphs: 1 Then the LORD said to Moses,
2 "Give this command to the people of Israel. When you enter the land of Canaan, it will be your own land with these borders:
3 Your southern border will go from the Wilderness of Zin along the border of Edom. On the east, it will start from the end of the Salt Sea.
4 It will cross south of the Ascent of Akrabbim, go on to Zin, and pass south of Kadesh-barnea. Then it will go to Hazar-addar and on to Azmon.
5 From Azmon it will turn, join the Brook of Egypt, and end at the Sea.
6 Your western border will be the coast of the Great Sea. This will be your border on the west.
7 Your northern border will go from the Great Sea straight to Mount Hor.
8 From Mount Hor it will go to Lebo-hamath, and then to Zedad.
9 It will go on to Ziphron and end at Hazar-enan. This will be your border on the north.
10 Your eastern border will go straight from Hazar-enan to Shepham.
11 Then it will go down from Shepham to Riblah, on the east side of Ain. It will continue along the slopes east of the Sea of Chinnereth.
12 Then the border will go down along the Jordan and end at the Salt Sea. This will be your land, with borders on all sides."
13 So Moses gave this command to the people of Israel: "Divide this land by lot as your own. The LORD has commanded that it be given to the nine and a half tribes.
14 The tribes of Reuben and Gad, with the half-tribe of Manasseh, have already received their land.
15 These two and a half tribes have their land across the Jordan from Jericho, toward the east."
16 Then the LORD said to Moses,
17 "These are the names of the men who will divide the land for you: Eleazar the priest and Joshua son of Nun.
18 Choose one leader from each tribe to divide the land.
19 These are their names: Caleb son of Jephunneh, from the tribe of Judah;
20 Shemuel son of Ammihud, from the tribe of Simeon;
21 Elidad son of Chislon, from the tribe of Benjamin;
22 Bukki son of Jogli, a leader from the tribe of Dan;
23 Hanniel son of Ephod, a leader from the tribe of Manasseh son of Joseph;
24 Kemuel son of Shiphtan, a leader from the tribe of Ephraim;
25 Eli-zaphan son of Parnach, a leader from the tribe of Zebulun;
26 Paltiel son of Azzan, a leader from the tribe of Issachar;
27 Ahihud son of Shelomi, a leader from the tribe of Asher;
28 and Pedahel son of Ammihud, a leader from the tribe of Naphtali."
29 These are the men whom the LORD commanded to divide the land of Canaan among the people of Israel.

title: Numbers 35
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Numbers
paragraphs: 1 The LORD spoke to Moses again on the plains of Moab, by the Jordan River, across from Jericho:
2 "Command the Israelites to give the Levites cities to live in. Give them open fields around the cities too. These come from the land the Israelites will receive.
3 The Levites will live in the cities. The open fields will be for their cattle, sheep, and all their other animals.
4 The open fields you give the Levites will reach about 450 meters (a thousand cubits) out from the city wall on every side.

5 You will also measure about 900 meters (two thousand cubits) on each side of the city — east, south, west, and north. The city will sit in the middle. These will be the larger open fields for the cities.
6 Six of the cities you give the Levites must be cities of refuge. A person who kills someone may run there for safety. Give the Levites forty-two more cities as well.
7 In total, you will give the Levites forty-eight cities, with their open fields.
8 Take these cities from the land of the Israelites. Larger tribes will give more cities; smaller tribes will give fewer."
9 Then the LORD said to Moses,
10 "Speak to the Israelites. Tell them: When you cross the Jordan River into the land of Canaan,
11 choose some cities to be your cities of refuge. A person who kills someone by accident may run there for safety.
12 These cities will protect him from the avenger of blood. He will not die before he stands before the assembly for judgment.
13 The cities you choose will be your six cities of refuge.
14 Choose three cities on the east side of the Jordan River. Choose three more in the land of Canaan. They will all be cities of refuge.
15 These six cities will be a safe place for the Israelites, and also for any foreigner or visitor among them. Anyone who kills a person by accident may run there.
16 But if a person hits someone with an iron object and kills him, he is a murderer. The murderer must be put to death.
17 Or if a person has a stone in his hand large enough to kill, and he hits and kills another, he is a murderer. The murderer must be put to death.
18 If a person has a deadly wooden object in his hand, and he hits and kills another, he is a murderer. The murderer must be put to death.
19 The avenger of blood will put the murderer to death. When he finds him, he will kill him.
20 If a person pushes another with hate, or throws something at him on purpose and kills him,
21 or hits him in anger with his hand so that he dies, the one who hit him must be put to death. He is a murderer. When the avenger of blood finds him, he will kill him.
22 But what if a person pushes someone suddenly, without hate? Or what if he throws something at him by accident?
23 Or what if he drops a heavy stone without looking, and it kills the man? He was not an enemy and did not want to harm him.
24 Then the assembly must judge between the one who killed and the avenger of blood, by these rules.

25 The assembly must protect the person who killed from the avenger of blood. Then they will send him back to the city of refuge he ran to. He must live there until the death of the high priest, who was anointed with the holy oil.
26 But what if the person who killed ever goes outside the city of refuge he ran to,
27 and the avenger of blood finds him outside the city and kills him? Then the avenger will not be guilty of killing.
28 The person who killed must stay in his city of refuge until the high priest dies. Only after the high priest dies may he go back to his own land.
29 This will be a lasting rule for you, for all the generations to come, wherever you live.
30 If a person kills someone, the murderer must be put to death only if witnesses speak against him. No one may be put to death because of just one witness.
31 Do not accept a payment to set free a murderer who deserves to die. He must be put to death.
32 Do not accept a payment for a person who has run to a city of refuge. Do not let him go home and live on his own land before the high priest dies.
33 Do not make the land where you live unclean. Killing makes the land unclean. No atonement can be made for land where blood was shed, except by the blood of the one who shed it.
34 Do not make the land unclean where you live, where I also live. For I, the LORD, live among the Israelites."

title: Numbers 36
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Numbers
paragraphs: 1 The leaders of the clan of Gilead came to Moses. Gilead was the son of Machir, son of Manasseh, from Joseph's family. They spoke to Moses and the other leaders of Israel.
2 They said, "The LORD told you to give the land to Israel by drawing lots. He also told you to give our brother Zelophehad's land to his daughters.
3 But if they marry men from other tribes of Israel, their land will go to that tribe. So the land we received will be taken away.
4 When the Year of Jubilee comes, their land will stay with the tribe they married into. It will be taken away from our tribe forever."
5 So by the word of the LORD, Moses gave this command to Israel: "The tribe of Joseph's sons is right.
6 This is what the LORD commands about Zelophehad's daughters: They may marry anyone they want. But they must marry inside their father's tribe.
7 No land in Israel may move from one tribe to another. Every Israelite must keep the land of his fathers' tribe.
8 Every daughter who owns land in any tribe of Israel must marry inside her father's tribe. Then every Israelite will keep his fathers' land.
9 No land may move from one tribe to another. Each tribe of Israel must keep its own land."
10 So Zelophehad's daughters did what the LORD commanded Moses.
11 Mahlah, Tirzah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Noah, the daughters of Zelophehad, married their cousins from their father's family.
12 They married inside the family line of Manasseh, son of Joseph. So their land stayed inside their father's clan.
13 These are the commands and rules that the LORD gave Israel through Moses. He gave them on the plains of Moab, by the Jordan River, across from Jericho.
